description:
---
references:
  uat: bestiary-43ib
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  prior_fix_plan: bestiary-dftg
  github_issue: https://github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary/issues/3
---
## Scope (UAT-5 REVISE — Component 3 layout ONLY)
ONE slice, ONE worker, ONE 3-axis review round. Layout/format change to the
ErrAmbiguous CLI output. No parser/InferFamilyFromID/Modifier work.

Verbatim user feedback (UAT-5 Q4):
"The ` *` marker should be a prefix, not a suffix. Lets show 5 canonical provider
models, and then maximum 5 distinct rehosts, with just the rehost provider name
listed. Dont need to show rehosts if none exist. Instructions should also given to
see all rehosts, or all providers/variants in general." (+ Q2: add a legend line.)

## Required changes
1. Marker `*` becomes a PREFIX: `* anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05` (not trailing).
2. Legend line explaining `*` (e.g. `* = canonical provider`).
3. Two-section layout in FormatAmbiguous:
   - Section 1: up to 5 canonical-provider model rows (the `*`-prefixed rows).
   - Section 2: up to 5 DISTINCT rehosts, by just the provider NAME (not full rows).
   - Omit Section 2 entirely if no rehosts exist.
4. Footer: accurate instructions to (a) see all rehosts for a model, (b) see all
   providers/variants in general. Use ONLY real commands (verified):
   `bestiary list`, `bestiary list --provider <slug>`, `bestiary show <id> --format=raw`.
   Do NOT invent flags.

## KEY DESIGN — data reshaping required (resolve.go)
ErrAmbiguous.Candidates is one canonical-preferred representative per distinct
(Family,Variant,Date) group — so rehost providers that ALSO host an anthropic-hosted
model (deepinfra, azure-cognitive-services, nano-gpt, perplexity-agent...) are NOT in
Candidates. The distinct-rehost list MUST be computed from the FULL match set.
Add `RehostProviders []Provider` to ErrAmbiguous (errors.go), populated by resolve.go
from `matches` = { m.Provider : m.Provider != m.Family.CanonicalProvider() }, deduped,
stable order. Keep Candidates semantics + ErrAmbiguous.Error() output UNCHANGED
(errors_test.go must still pass). PURL missed-namespace note (Fix 2) unchanged.

## Slices
- SLICE-FIX-V4-1: data field + populate (resolve.go/errors.go) + FormatAmbiguous
  two-section render + tests. Single file cluster, no cross-slice integration points.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `bestiary show claude`: legend line; up to 5 `*`-prefixed canonical (anthropic) rows;
   then a distinct-rehost section (<=5 provider names) since rehosts exist; then footer
   instructions for all-rehosts and all-providers/variants.
2. A family with NO rehosts omits the rehost section cleanly.
3. `*` is a PREFIX on the canonical entry, not a suffix.
4. Fix 2 PURL missed-namespace note STILL prints unchanged.
5. `bestiary show claude/opus@2025-11-24` -> Provider=anthropic; `--format purl
   pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5` -> anthropic. No Resolve regressions.
6. Tests updated/added (prefix marker, two-section split, rehost-omitted case, legend,
   footer). `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...` + `go vet ./...` pass.

## Scope guard
C3 layout ONLY. Do NOT touch parser, InferFamilyFromID, Modifier extraction, empty-
RawFamily mangling (bestiary-4yuy). If the worker proposes parser changes, STOP them.
title: FIX_IMPL_PLAN_V4: Component 3 ambiguous-output layout rework (UAT-5 REVISE)

description:

references:
  slice: bestiary-hf4
  reviewer: reviewer-B
  round: 1
  severity_group: bestiary-aot2
---
## Finding

The shared testModel() helper in store_test.go constructs ModelInfo with zero values for NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant, and NormalizedDate. All store round-trip tests (insert → read back), merge tests, and the schema_test.go fixture also leave these three new fields unset.

Consequence: no automated test currently proves that:
- Store.Save() + Store.All() round-trips NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date correctly
- MergeModels() preserves normalized fields under most-recent-wins semantics
- FormatModel JSON output includes normalized fields with non-zero values

schema_test.go step-5/6 would catch a missing JSON key only if the fixture had non-zero values. As it stands the test accepts empty string ('') for all three fields, which satisfies the schema property check but doesn't prove the fields contain useful data.

## Why MINOR (not IMPORTANT)
The schema JSON property conformance check still passes. The fields are zero-value but present. This is a test hygiene gap rather than a correctness risk.
title: MINOR: testModel() fixture does not include NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date; store/merge round-trips omit normalized fields

close_reason: Fixed in commit 4faf1c3: renamed to TestMigration_FreshDB_Idempotent (tests v3 idempotency); added new TestMigration_V2Idempotent that starts from a createV2DB fixture and opens twice to verify the v2->v3 migration path is idempotent.
description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-est
  reviewer: B
  round: 1
  file: store_migration_test.go
  test: TestMigration_V2Idempotent
---
## Finding

TestMigration_V2Idempotent (store_migration_test.go, line ~229) has a stale comment:
  // First open — creates fresh v2 DB and writes a row.

But OpenStore() now always creates a v3 DB (currentSchemaVersion = 3), not a v2 DB.
The second open therefore tests v3 idempotency, not v2 idempotency.

Additionally the test name 'V2Idempotent' is misleading: it was not changed from the original
v2-era test despite the chain change in commit 9feddbd. A reader expects this test to exercise
a v2 DB opened a second time without re-migration; instead it tests that a v3 DB opened twice
stays at v3 — which is already covered by TestMigration_V2toV3_Idempotent.

There is now a gap: no test starts with a v2 DB on disk and opens it twice to verify idempotency
of the v2→v3 migration path (i.e., that running migration twice does not corrupt data).

## Suggested fix

Rename to TestMigration_V3Idempotent and update the comment, OR rewrite it to use createV2DB
so it genuinely tests v2 idempotency (first open: v2→v3 migration; second open: already v3, no re-migration).
title: IMPORTANT: TestMigration_V2Idempotent comment/name stale — test now creates v3 DB, not v2

close_reason: Fixed in commit b507411: update _comment in variant_suffixes.json to say order is irrelevant at runtime, initParseData sorts automatically
description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-n2q
  reviewer: reviewer-C
  round: 1
---
## Finding

`parse/data/variant_suffixes.json` contains this `_comment`:

> "Longest entries must come first to avoid premature short-match."

However, `initParseData()` re-sorts the suffixes by length at runtime (`sort.Slice` by `len`). The JSON ordering is therefore **irrelevant to correctness** — the runtime sort is the authority.

This misleads future data file editors: they may spend time maintaining the "correct" JSON order when it does not matter, or they may trust that JSON order controls behavior and be surprised when it doesn't.

## Suggested fix

Update the `_comment` to:

> "List of variant suffix patterns (with leading hyphen). Order in this file does not affect runtime behavior — initParseData sorts suffixes longest-first automatically."

This is a documentation-only fix with no code change.
title: MINOR: variant_suffixes.json _comment misleads about ordering being authoritative

---
references:
  request: bestiary-n6x
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  elicit: bestiary-73x
  proposal: bestiary-1oq
  handoff: bestiary-28c

---

## Objective
Decompose the ratified PROPOSAL-3 (bestiary-1oq) into vertical slices with explicit horizontal Layer Integration Points. Each slice owns a full vertical (types → tests → impl → wiring) and is implemented by exactly one worker.

## Decomposition: 8 Vertical Slices

### Slice 1 — parse package + codegen template apply (foundation)
Owner files (NEW): `parse.go`, `parse_test.go`, `parse/data/family_overrides.json`, `parse/data/variant_suffixes.json`, `parse/data/version_patterns.json`.
Touched: `families_gen.go`, `models_static_gen.go`, `providers_gen.go` (regenerate via `go generate ./...` to apply commit 6a8d75b template fix — must end with `type Family string`, NOT `type Family = string`). `.bestiary-gen-cache/api_response.json` (dirty, will be re-cached).

Deliverables:
- `ParseFamily(raw Family) (Family, string)` — uses overrides table → suffix table → versioned-variant patterns (v/k/m/no-prefix). Hyphen-separated versions (claude-opus-4-5) handled. Empty raw families return zero values.
- `ExtractDate(id ModelID, releaseDate string) string` — match YYYYMMDD or YYYY-MM-DD only.
- `inferFamilyFromID(id ModelID, p Provider) Family` — fallback for empty-family models (~25%).
- Embed JSON via `//go:embed parse/data/*.json` (regular package directory, NOT testdata/).
- `go generate ./...` regenerates all `_gen.go` files with named Family type.

### Slice 2 — ModelInfo extension + family methods + codegen normalization splice
Owner files (NEW): `family.go`, `family_test.go`.
Touched: `bestiary.go` (ModelInfo new fields), `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` (genToModelInfo at line 524, validateGeneratedFamilyType post-condition).

Deliverables:
- ModelInfo gains `NormalizedFamily Family`, `NormalizedVariant string`, `NormalizedDate string`.
- `family.go`: `Family.IsKnown()`, `String()`, `MarshalText()`, `UnmarshalText()` — resolves debt task bestiary-ptm.
- Codegen splice: invoke `parse.ParseFamily` + `parse.ExtractDate` in `genToModelInfo()` to bake Normalized fields into `models_static_gen.go`.
- `validateGeneratedFamilyType(path)` post-condition runs at end of `run()` and asserts grep for `type Family string` (no `=`).
- TestFamilyType_NamedNotAlias regression test in package root.

### Slice 3 — ModelRef + canonical + designation + Resolve (public API)
Owner files (NEW): `canonical.go`, `designation.go`, `resolve.go`, `canonical_test.go`, `designation_test.go`, `resolve_test.go`.
Touched: `modelref.go` (ModelRef gains ID field, becomes 6-field; Format(scheme) dispatch; Designations() method; Stringer uses SchemeCanonical), `errors.go` (NEW *ErrAmbiguous struct error), `errors_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- `CanonicalScheme` int enum: SchemeCanonical | SchemeHuggingFace | SchemePURL | SchemeRaw (UAT Option A formats).
- `Designation{Value, Scheme, Provider, Rating}`, `AcceptabilityRating` int enum (Admitted | Preferred | Deprecated). All static designations default to Admitted.
- ModelRef 6 fields: ID, Provider, RawFamily, Family, Variant, Date.
- ModelRef.Format(scheme), ModelRef.Designations().
- `Resolve(input string, opts ...ResolveOption) ([]ModelRef, error)` — slice for cross-provider hosting, *ErrAmbiguous for distinct canonicals, ErrNotFound for zero. WithScheme(s) option.

### Slice 4 — Model_ constants generation
Owner files (NEW): `cmd/bestiary-gen` additions for `generateConstantsSource()`, `models_constants_gen.go` (generated), `constants_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- New codegen step: emits `Model_<Provider>_<Family>_<Variant>_<Date>?` constants. Underscore-separated component naming. Provider/Family/Harness keep PascalCase.
- Two-pass collision algorithm: append disambiguator from raw ID version segment, fall back to `_<n>` sequential suffix.
- Skip rule: empty family AND no extractable family from ID → no constant generated.
- `Models() []ModelID` returns all generated constants (defensive copy).
- Tests: TestModelConstants_Unique, TestModelConstants_RoundTrip, TestModelConstants_NamingStable.

### Slice 5 — SQLite v2→v3 migration + QueryByCanonical
Owner files (NEW): `migrations/v2_to_v3.sql`.
Touched: `store.go` (migrateToV3 mirroring migrateToV2 pattern, currentSchemaVersion bump, scanModelInfo + UpsertModels column-list updates, new QueryByCanonical method), `store_migration_test.go`, `store_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- v2→v3 migration: rename existing `family` → `raw_family`; add `family` (parsed), `variant`, `date` columns; create `idx_canonical ON models(family, variant, provider)`.
- Backfill on first migrate: re-run parse logic on existing rows.
- QueryByCanonical(ctx, family, variant, date) ([]ModelInfo, error).
- Standalone SQL file at `migrations/v2_to_v3.sql` containing equivalent ALTER/CREATE statements (reference doc, not executed by Go code).
- Migration test: 5 sub-tests — preserves-data, backfill, idempotent, edge-cases, index-used.

### Slice 6 — CLI extensions (bestiary show + bestiary-gen cache flags)
Touched: `cmd/bestiary/main.go` (show: --scheme=<canonical|huggingface|purl|raw> flag, auto-detect by prefix, ErrAmbiguous candidate-list rendering), `cmd/bestiary/main_test.go`, `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` (--cache-dir <path> and --no-fetch flags), `cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- `bestiary show <input> [--scheme=...]` resolves via Resolve. Auto-detect default. Prints candidate table on *ErrAmbiguous.
- `bestiary-gen --cache-dir <path>` overrides hardcoded `.bestiary-gen-cache/`.
- `bestiary-gen --no-fetch` reads cached api_response.json instead of HTTP. Cache miss → actionable error (path, reason, remediation per C-actionable-errors).

### Slice 7 — Schema + version + MIGRATION docs
Touched: `bestiary.schema.json` (document NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date, ModelRef.ID, Designation array, AcceptabilityRating enum, schema version 0.0.2), `version.go` (BestiarySchemaVersion → "0.0.2", NOT 0.1.0), `schema_test.go`, `version_test.go`. NEW: `MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md`.

Deliverables:
- BestiarySchemaVersion exact string "0.0.2".
- bestiary.schema.json conformance test (TestJSONOutput_ConformsToSchema) passes against new fields.
- MIGRATION doc: ModelInfo new fields, ModelRef.ID, parse package, SQL column renames, Model_ constants naming, schema version bump.

### Slice 8 — Provenance companion PR (separate repo)
Repo: `~/codebases/dayvidpham/provenance/` (NOTE: repo root, not under `/main/`).
Touched: `pkg/ptypes/enums.go` (Provider.IsValid delegates to bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown(); MarshalText/UnmarshalText permissive), `pkg/ptypes/enums_test.go`, `models_test.go`, `go.mod` (bump bestiary dependency to v0.0.2 — note: current pin is v0.1.0; if release tag differs from BestiarySchemaVersion, surface back to supervisor).

Deliverables:
- Provider remains a wrapper string type but loses 4-value enforcement.
- RegistryFromBestiary unchanged (already a direct cast).
- Tests refactored to drop 4-value assumption; add 110-provider validation case.
- PR ready for coordinated landing once bestiary v0.0.2 is tagged.

## Horizontal Layer Integration Points

| ID | Owner | Consumers | Contract | Merge Timing |
|----|-------|-----------|----------|--------------|
| IP-1 | Slice 1 | All slices | `families_gen.go` declares `type Family string` (named type) | After Slice 1 commits & before Slice 2 starts |
| IP-2 | Slice 2 | Slices 3, 4, 5, 7 | `ModelInfo.NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date` populated by codegen | After Slice 2 commits |
| IP-3 | Slice 3 | Slices 6, 7 | `ModelRef` 6-field shape with `ID ModelID`; `CanonicalScheme` enum; `*ErrAmbiguous` | After Slice 3 commits |
| IP-4 | Slice 1 | Slices 2, 4, 6 | `parse.ParseFamily`, `parse.ExtractDate`, `parse.InferFamilyFromID` public API | After Slice 1 commits |
| IP-5 | Slice 5 | Slice 7 | `family`/`variant`/`date` columns; `idx_canonical` index | After Slice 5 commits |
| IP-6 | Slice 4 | None internal; external Models() | `Model_*` constants + `Models()` API | After Slice 4 commits |
| IP-7 | Slice 7 | Slice 8 | `BestiarySchemaVersion = "0.0.2"` ratified; ready for tag | After Slice 7 commits |

## Build Wave Order

- **Wave 1 (parallel)**: Slice 1, Slice 8 (Slice 8 is independent of bestiary internals; only its go.mod bump waits for v0.0.2 tag).
- **Wave 2 (depends on Slice 1)**: Slice 2.
- **Wave 3 (depends on Slice 2, parallel)**: Slice 3, Slice 4, Slice 5.
- **Wave 4 (depends on Slice 3)**: Slice 6.
- **Wave 5 (depends on Slice 2-5)**: Slice 7.

## Acceptance Criteria (from HANDOFF bestiary-28c)
See full list in handoff. All slices must collectively satisfy:
- `go vet ./...` and `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...` pass.
- families_gen.go declares `type Family string` (named type), confirmed by TestFamilyType_NamedNotAlias.
- BestiarySchemaVersion exact string "0.0.2".
- MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md exists.
- migrations/v2_to_v3.sql exists.
- bestiary.schema.json conformance test passes.

## Cross-cutting reminders
- All commits via `git agent-commit -m "..."` (NEVER `git commit`).
- Provider/Family/Harness keep PascalCase (no breaking change). Only Model_* constants use underscore separators.
- Schema 0.0.1 → 0.0.2 (NOT 0.1.0).

description:
---
references:
  request: bestiary-64s
  elicit: bestiary-bl1
---

## Requirements

### R1: Strongly-typed Provider with string extensibility
- Provider is a `string` type with well-known constants (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, Local)
- `IsKnown()` method for checking well-known providers
- Any models.dev provider slug is a valid Provider value
- ProviderLocal = "local" is a bestiary-specific concept (not from API)

### R2: Comprehensive ModelInfo capturing all 17 API fields
- All fields from models.dev API: ID, Provider, DisplayName, Family, ContextWindow, MaxOutput
- Boolean capabilities: Reasoning, ToolCall, Attachment, Temperature, StructuredOutput, Interleaved, OpenWeights
- Pricing: 5 cost fields as *float64 (genuinely optional)
- Metadata: ReleaseDate, Knowledge (cutoff date) as string
- Modalities: typed enum sets (Modality int with Text/Image/PDF/Audio/Video constants)

### R3: Typed Modality enum
- `type Modality int` with constants: ModalityText, ModalityImage, ModalityPDF, ModalityAudio, ModalityVideo
- Modalities struct with `Input []Modality` and `Output []Modality`
- String/MarshalText/UnmarshalText methods

### R4: Code-generated static model registry (~110 models)
- go:generate command fetches models.dev/api.json and writes models_static_gen.go
- Covers all models from anthropic, google, openai providers
- Generated file committed to git
- StaticModels() returns the compiled-in data
- LookupModel(), ModelsByProvider(), ModelsByFamily() query helpers

### R5: HTTP client with functional options
- NewClient(opts ...ClientOption) pattern
- Configurable: timeout, retries, base URL
- Retry: 2 retries with exponential backoff
- Response limit: 10 MB
- FetchModels(ctx) returns all providers from API
- FetchModelsByProvider(ctx, p) filters by provider

### R6: Structured error types
- ErrNotFound for lookup misses
- ErrAPIUnavailable for network/API failures
- Actionable error messages (what, why, where, how to fix)

### R7: CLI with JSON and YAML output
- bestiary list [--provider X] [--format json|yaml|table]
- bestiary show <model-id> [--format json|yaml|table]
- bestiary sync [--provider X] [--format json|yaml]
- Default output format: JSON
- Table format as human-readable option

### R8: No external dependencies beyond stdlib
- CGO_ENABLED=0
- stdlib-only (encoding/json, net/http, flag or internal CLI)
- Exception: YAML output may need a library OR use a simple serializer

## Priorities
1. Importable Go bindings (Provider, ModelInfo, registry) — provenance needs this first
2. HTTP client — fetch live data
3. CLI — developer UX for querying
4. Code generation tooling — keep static data current

## Design Choices
- Hybrid Provider (string type + constants) over closed enum — extensibility for 109 providers
- All 17 fields over minimal 12 — future-proof, captures full API
- Typed Modality enum over string slices — type safety consistent with Provider design
- Codegen over hand-maintained static data — 110 models is impractical to hand-maintain
- Functional options over config struct — idiomatic Go, extensible
- ProviderLocal as bestiary concept — empty for now, placeholder for future local model support

## MVP Goals
- Package exports: Provider, ModelID, ModelInfo, Modality, Modalities types
- Registry: StaticModels(), LookupModel(), ModelsByProvider(), ModelsByFamily()
- Client: NewClient(), FetchModels(), FetchModelsByProvider()
- CLI: list, show, sync subcommands with JSON/YAML/table output
- Codegen: go:generate for static model data
- Tests: registry tests, client tests with mock HTTP, static fallback tests

## End-Vision Goals
- Canonical Go module for LLM model metadata
- Used by provenance to replace hardcoded ml_models seed SQL
- Extensible to all 109 providers over time
- ProviderLocal populated with curated local model entries
- Potential: caching layer, rate limiting, webhook for model updates
title: URD: bestiary Go module

references:

## Specification (Component 6)

UAT-2 Component 6 REVISE: URD R9 explicitly required `provenance.Provider.IsValid()` to delegate to `bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown()`. The current state in `pkg/ptypes/enums.go:306` and `:346` says "leaf-package layering; no parent-package import" and only does a non-empty check. **Rejected.**

The fix lifts validation OUT of `pkg/ptypes` and into a higher provenance layer (`adapter.go`, the root provenance package, where bestiary IS imported). `pkg/ptypes` exposes only the Provider type; validation routes through the higher-level layer.

## Repo

**Working directory**: `/home/minttea/codebases/dayvidpham/provenance`

NOT the bestiary repo. Operate on the provenance repo.

## Files Owned (this slice's vertical, in provenance repo)

- `pkg/ptypes/enums.go` — strip the rejected `Provider.IsValid()` method (or repurpose as a clearly-non-binding stub); remove the "leaf-package layering" comments at lines ~306, ~345.
- `adapter.go` — add `IsValid(p ptypes.Provider) bool` (function, lifted from method) that does (1) non-empty check + (2) bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown(). The existing IsKnown(p) function stays.
- `pkg/ptypes/enums_test.go` — remove or rewrite TestProviderIsValid and TestProvider_WhitespaceUnmarshal to match the new architecture.
- `adapter_test.go` (new file at provenance root) — table-driven tests for provenance.IsValid covering: known providers, case-sensitive miss, unknown but non-empty, empty, whitespace.
- `cmd/demo/main.go:34` — update from method call to function call.
- `demo_test.go:553-557` — update assertions.
- Any internal callers of IsValid discovered via grep — migrate them.

## Decision: API shape (binding)

- Remove pkg/ptypes.Provider.IsValid() method entirely (clean break).
- Add provenance.IsValid(p ptypes.Provider) bool in adapter.go.
- Breaking change for external callers; document in CHANGELOG/release notes for next provenance tag.
- If the worker discovers a strong reason to use a wrapping-type approach (provenance.Provider wrapping ptypes.Provider with method override), flag back to supervisor before deviating.

## Behaviors

- B1: provenance.IsValid(p) returns false for empty/whitespace strings.
- B2: provenance.IsValid(p) returns true iff bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown() returns true (case-sensitive).
- B3: pkg/ptypes/enums.go no longer references the rejected layering rationale; comments removed.
- B4: pkg/ptypes still imports nothing beyond stdlib.
- B5: All callers of the previous IsValid() method are migrated.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Given provenance.IsValid(ptypes.Provider(\"anthropic\")), when called, then return true.
- Given provenance.IsValid(ptypes.Provider(\"ANTHROPIC\")), when called, then return false (case-sensitive).
- Given provenance.IsValid(ptypes.Provider(\"\")), when called, then return false.
- Given provenance.IsValid(ptypes.Provider(\"  \\t  \")), when called, then return false.
- Given provenance.IsValid(ptypes.Provider(\"amazon-bedrock\")) (not in bestiary), when called, then return false.
- Given pkg/ptypes/enums.go, when reading, then no comment mentions \"leaf-package layering\".
- Given CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./... and go vet ./... in the provenance repo, when run, then all pass.

## Release Coordination (NOT this worker's responsibility)

- The bestiary repo is NOT pushed yet. The bestiary fix wave lands first.
- After bestiary slices land + new tag, supervisor surfaces version-bump strategy to team-lead.
- Provenance go.mod gets fresh bestiary pin only AFTER bestiary tag.
- Worker commits provenance fix locally; do NOT push.

## Leaf Tasks
- SLICE-FIX-4-L1: Types (move IsValid out of ptypes; add adapter signature)
- SLICE-FIX-4-L2: Tests (adapter_test.go table-driven; update existing enums_test.go)
- SLICE-FIX-4-L3: Impl (adapter.go IsValid body; comment cleanup; caller migration)

---
references:

# Handoff: Architect → Supervisor

## Task IDs

| Role | Task ID | Title |
|------|---------|-------|
| REQUEST | bestiary-64s | Implement dayvidpham/bestiary Go module + CLI |
| URD | bestiary-0pi | User Requirements Document |
| RATIFIED PROPOSAL | bestiary-tgf | PROPOSAL-2 (ratified) |
| ELICIT | bestiary-bl1 | URE survey results |
| RESEARCH | bestiary-7iw | models.dev API analysis |
| UAT | bestiary-aat | Plan UAT results |

## Context

bestiary is a Go module (github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary) + CLI that wraps the
https://models.dev/api.json API. It provides:
1. Importable Go bindings — strongly-typed providers, model IDs, static model registry
2. HTTP client — fetches and parses models.dev API with retry
3. SQLite cache — persists synced models for offline list/show
4. CLI — bestiary sync, bestiary list, bestiary show with JSON/YAML/table output
5. Codegen — go:generate tool fetches API and writes static model data

Downstream consumer: dayvidpham/provenance will go get github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary
and call bestiary.StaticModels() to replace hardcoded ml_models seed SQL.

## Key Decisions

1. Provider: type Provider string with 4 well-known constants (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, Local). IsKnown() method. Any provider slug from API is valid.
2. ModelInfo: All 17 API fields. LastSynced field for merge timestamp. Cost fields as *float64, others as zero-value types.
3. Modality: type Modality int enum with 5 constants (Text, Image, PDF, Audio, Video). Bounds-checked String().
4. Static data: Codegen from API (~110 models from 3 providers). go:generate go run ./cmd/bestiary-gen.
5. SQLite cache: zombiezen.com/go/sqlite (CGO-free). DB at XDG_CACHE_HOME/bestiary/models.db, --db-path override.
6. Merge: MergeModels(static, cached) — deduplicate by ModelID, most-recent LastSynced wins.
7. CLI behavior: list/show=offline (static + cache), sync=online (fetches and persists).
8. YAML: Internal minimal serializer (~50 lines, write-only). No external YAML dep.
9. Client: Functional options pattern. 10 MB response limit. 2 retries with backoff.
10. Errors: Struct types ErrNotFound, ErrAPIUnavailable (use errors.As, not errors.Is).

## Open Items

None — all decisions resolved through URE + UAT.

## Implementation Slices (Suggested by Architect)

### Slice 1: Core Types (Layer 0)
- bestiary.go, provider.go, modality.go, errors.go
- go.mod with zombiezen.com/go/sqlite

### Slice 2: Wire Types + Client
- wire.go, client.go, client_test.go
- 10 MB limit, retry, context cancellation

### Slice 3: SQLite Store
- store.go, store_test.go
- Schema, UpsertModels, QueryModels, DefaultDBPath

### Slice 4: Codegen + Static Registry
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go, models_static_gen.go
- registry.go, registry_test.go

### Slice 5: Merge + Format + CLI
- merge.go, merge_test.go, format.go, format_test.go
- cmd/bestiary/main.go with --db-path

## Acceptance Criteria

See PROPOSAL-2 (bestiary-tgf) design field for full validation checklist (22 items)
and acceptance criteria (7 BDD scenarios).

## Source Material

Extract and adapt from ~/dev/agent-data-leverage/develop/:
- internal/store/models_data.go — static model data pattern
- internal/store/models_client.go — HTTP client with retry
- internal/ingest/store_iface.go:138-154 — original ModelInfo struct

## Constraints

- Go 1.24+, CGO_ENABLED=0
- Dependencies: stdlib + zombiezen.com/go/sqlite only
- Use git agent-commit for commits
- Model IDs use hyphens (matching models.dev): claude-opus-4-6, NOT claude_opus_4

## Planning Phases Complete

- Phase 1: REQUEST captured and classified (bestiary-64s)
- Phase 2: URE completed (bestiary-bl1), URD created (bestiary-0pi)
- Phase 3: PROPOSAL-1 (bestiary-j5r, superseded) then PROPOSAL-2 (bestiary-tgf)
- Phase 4: 3 reviewers ACCEPT PROPOSAL-2 (bestiary-xny, bestiary-93h, bestiary-j35)
- Phase 5: UAT-1 REVISED (bestiary-aat) — added SQLite cache layer
- Phase 6: PROPOSAL-2 ratified
- Phase 7: This handoff

## Specification
Two surgical fixes to Component 3 ambiguous-result rendering. Same file cluster.

### Fix 1 — Canonical-provider distinction in the ErrAmbiguous table
When a Family has a canonical provider (Family.CanonicalProvider() != ""), the
ambiguous output must surface that provider rows AND visually distinguish them
from rehosts. Two coordinated edits:

(a) resolve.go (~line 255-261, ambiguous candidate assembly): when picking the
    representative ModelRef per (Family,Variant,Date) group, PREFER the row whose
    Provider == ref.Family.CanonicalProvider() if such a row exists in that group;
    otherwise keep byGroup[key][0]. This makes Anthropic rows APPEAR as the
    representative wherever Anthropic hosts the model. Do NOT change the cross-provider
    single-group path (resolve.go:241-250) or the PURL loose-fallback candidate build.

(b) format.go FormatAmbiguous (~line 321): visually distinguish canonical rows.
    Recommended (worker has design latitude — justify your pick in a bd comment):
      - Sort canonical-provider rows to the TOP before truncation (so they survive
        the N=10 cut), AND
      - Add a visible marker distinguishing canonical from rehost — e.g. a 4th
        "canonical" column / a trailing "*" / a "(canonical)" tag. A row is canonical
        when row.Provider == row.Family.CanonicalProvider().
    Extend the EXISTING grouping (by Family,Variant,Version) and truncation
    (N=10 + "+M more") — do NOT rebuild them.

### Fix 2 — Surface the PURL loose-fallback missed-namespace note
In FormatAmbiguous, when e.PURLMissedNamespace != "", print a note BEFORE/ABOVE the
candidate table, matching the errors.go wording:
  "no matches in namespace %q — performing loose match across all providers"
Keep ErrAmbiguous.Error() behavior unchanged (both paths show it).

## Files Owned
- resolve.go (candidate-assembly representative selection only)
- format.go (FormatAmbiguous rendering)
- format_test.go (new/updated tests)
- resolve_test.go (new/updated tests if needed)

## Scope (Layer 3 — make L2 tests pass)

### parse.go ExtractModifier
- Load modifiers from parse/data/modifiers.json (use longest-suffix-first matching to avoid `-think` shadowing `-thinking` if both seeded)
- After family + variant resolved, scan id for trailing `-<modifier>` token where modifier is in the allowlist
- Return (modifier, consumed) where consumed is the trailing substring including the leading hyphen

### parse.go integration
- Update ParseFamilyWithVersion (or its codegen caller) to call ExtractModifier AFTER variant resolution
- Strip consumed substring from id before passing to ExtractVersionFromID and ExtractDate
- Ensure ParseFamilyDetailed (V2-3) still emits ParseFailure for non-modifier overflow (Mode 1, ReasonUnknownSuffixOverflow)

### canonical.go
- Format: emit `[modifier]` suffix when ref.Modifier != ""
- Parse: regex/lex to consume optional `[...]` suffix at end before parsing date/version

### format.go
- JSON marshal emits Modifier (since ModelInfo has no explicit json: tags, default field name applies)
- YAML serializer adds Modifier line (omit or empty per existing pattern for empty optional fields)
- Table renderer adds Modifier column (or extends existing column layout)

### cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go
- Constant-name template adds __Modifier__ slot between version and date
- Apply casing override to modifier tokens (Thinking, Vision, Latest, Code, Preview, etc.) — extend the existing casing override table from V2-1/V2-2

  - Document the new field, ExtractModifier function, modifiers.json
  - Document canonical bracket-suffix change (with examples)

## Specification
Rework the ErrAmbiguous CLI output per UAT-5 verbatim feedback. Same file cluster.

### Data layer (resolve.go + errors.go)
Add `RehostProviders []Provider` field to ErrAmbiguous (errors.go struct + doc comment).
In resolve.go, where ErrAmbiguous is constructed for the multi-group case (~line 262-267)
AND the PURL loose-fallback case (~line 173-179), populate RehostProviders = distinct
providers from the FULL `matches` set where m.Provider != m.Family.CanonicalProvider(),
deduped, in stable (first-seen) order. Keep Candidates UNCHANGED and Error() output
UNCHANGED (errors_test.go must still pass).

### Render layer (format.go FormatAmbiguous)
New layout (replace the single-table block; KEEP the PURLMissedNamespace note at top):
- Header: `bestiary: input %q matched multiple canonicals`
- Legend: `* = canonical provider`
- Section 1 "Canonical:" — up to 5 canonical rows (Provider == Family.CanonicalProvider()),
  each rendered with a `*` PREFIX (e.g. `* anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05`).
  If >5 canonical rows exist, show a brief "+N more" style hint.
- Section 2 "Also rehosted by:" — up to 5 DISTINCT rehost provider NAMES from
  RehostProviders (NOT full canonical rows). If >5, "+N more". OMIT this section entirely
  when RehostProviders is empty.
- Footer instructions (use ONLY verified real commands):
  - see all providers/variants: `bestiary list` (or `bestiary list --provider <slug>`)
  - resolve an exact model ID across the registry: `bestiary show <raw-id> --format=raw`
  Do NOT invent flags. Verify each command runs.

Suggested constants: ambiguousMaxCanonical = 5, ambiguousMaxRehosts = 5 (replace/augment
the existing ambiguousMaxCandidates = 10).

## Files Owned
- errors.go (add RehostProviders field only; do NOT change Error() output)
- resolve.go (populate RehostProviders in both ErrAmbiguous construction sites)
- format.go (FormatAmbiguous rewrite)
- format_test.go, resolve_test.go, errors_test.go (tests)

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] Section 1 <=5 canonical rows; Section 2 <=5 distinct rehost names; omitted when none
- [ ] Footer instructions use real commands (verified to run)
- [ ] Fix 2 PURL note unchanged; errors_test.go passes
- [ ] No Resolve regressions (claude/opus@2025-11-24 + purl anthropic -> anthropic)
- [ ] go test ./... + go vet ./... pass

## OUT OF SCOPE
- parser, InferFamilyFromID, Modifier extraction, empty-RawFamily (bestiary-4yuy). STOP and notify supervisor if a fix seems to need parser changes.

close_reason: Fixed in commit 5d7f05f: removed redundant 'What:' label from ErrCacheMiss.Error() (leading line already serves as What description)
description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ektc
  reviewer: C
  round: 1
  file: cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go
  lines: "449-460"
---
## Finding

ErrCacheMiss.Error() opens with:

  'cached api_response.json missing or empty\n  What: cached api_response.json missing or empty\n'

The first line and the 'What:' label are identical. For a user reading the error in a terminal, this reads as a stutter: the same sentence appears twice in two consecutive lines.

## Impact

Minor readability regression. The C-actionable-errors template asks for the summary first (that's the leading line), then the structured labels. The leading line IS the 'What', so repeating it as a labelled field is redundant.

## Suggestion

Either:
  (a) remove 'What: ...' from the labelled list (the first line already serves that role), OR
  (b) make the leading line a short one-liner (e.g. 'cache miss: %s') and use 'What: cached api_response.json missing or empty' as the structured label.

Option (a) is simpler and consistent with how the other fmt.Errorf messages in this file handle it (they have a leading summary followed by specific structured labels that add NEW information).
title: MINOR: ErrCacheMiss.Error() duplicates the 'What' sentence as both the leading summary and a labelled field

## Changes from PROPOSAL-2

PROPOSAL-2 was ACCEPTed by Reviewers B and C, but REVISEd by Reviewer A who caught 3 new BLOCKERs from architectural changes.

### From Reviewer A Round 2 (REVISE — 3 new BLOCKERs, 1 IMPORTANT, 1 MINOR)

- **A-N1 BLOCKER**: `Format(SchemeRaw)` requires `ModelID`, which `ModelRef` does not carry.
  - **Fix**: Add `ID ModelID` field to ModelRef. Populated at codegen time alongside Family/Variant/Date.
- **A-N2 BLOCKER**: `testdata/family_overrides.json` and `testdata/variant_suffixes.json` cannot reach a production binary — `testdata/` is excluded by Go tooling and `go:embed`.
  - **Fix**: Move data to `decompose/data/family_overrides.json` and `decompose/data/variant_suffixes.json` (regular package directory, embeddable). Alternative considered: bake JSON content into generated Go literals at codegen time. Chose embed-based approach for runtime reload-ability.
- **A-N3 BLOCKER**: families_gen.go currently has `type Family = string` (alias) despite codegen template fix in commit `6a8d75b`. The template was fixed but the file was not regenerated.
  - **Fix**: Implementation MUST run `go generate ./...` to regenerate families_gen.go with the named type. Validation step: assert `grep "type Family string" families_gen.go` succeeds (no `=`).
- **A-N4 IMPORTANT**: `ModelInfo` gains 3 new exported fields (`NormalizedFamily`, `NormalizedVariant`, `NormalizedDate`) referenced only in code snippet.
  - **Fix**: Deliverable #5 explicitly enumerates new ModelInfo fields. bestiary.schema.json updated. SQLite store reads/writes them.
- **A-N5 MINOR**: Versioned-variant regex pattern uses `<base>-v<MAJOR>.<MINOR>` but actual data has `kimi-k2.5` (k prefix), `qwen3.5` (no prefix), `minimax-m2.5` (m prefix).
  - **Fix**: Versioned-variant rule revised to: `<base>-?<letter><MAJOR>(.<MINOR>)?` where letter ∈ {v, k, m, M} OR direct version pattern `<base><digits>.<digits>` (qwen3.5 case).
- **A-B6 STILL OPEN from Round 1**: Model constants spec still insufficient.
  - **Fix**: Deliverable #8 fully enumerates: naming convention, collision-resolution algorithm, generated file structure, test plan.

### From Reviewer C Round 2 (ACCEPT with 4 non-blocking notes)

- **C-N1**: Resolve `[]ModelRef` vs `ErrAmbiguous` distinction needs explicit doc rule.
  - **Fix**: Documented rule: Resolve returns `[]ModelRef` when 0+ matches share the same Canonical (cross-provider hosting); returns `*ErrAmbiguous` when multiple distinct Canonicals match (e.g., user typed "claude" matching opus AND sonnet AND haiku).
- **C-N2**: `Format(SchemeRaw)` needs ModelID — same as A-N1, addressed.
- **C-N3**: `SchemeDefault` is role-name not format-name.
  - **Fix**: Renamed `SchemeDefault` → `SchemeCanonical`. Better describes the format (slash-separated canonical form).
- **C-N4**: testdata/ unconventional for codegen input — addressed by A-N2 fix (moved to decompose/data/).

### From Reviewer B Round 2 (ACCEPT with 1 non-blocking note)

- **B-F4**: Constants tests committed in change-list but not enumerated under specific file.
  - **Fix**: Test Plan now lists `constants_test.go` with 3 named tests.

---

## Problem Space

(Same as PROPOSAL-1/2 — see those for context.)

## Engineering Tradeoffs (delta from PROPOSAL-2)

| Decision | PROPOSAL-2 choice | PROPOSAL-3 revision | Rationale |
|----------|-------------------|---------------------|-----------|
| ModelRef.ID | Implicit / not present | **Add ID ModelID field** | Reviewer A-N1: SchemeRaw can't format without it. |
| Decomp data location | testdata/ | **decompose/data/** | Reviewer A-N2: testdata/ is excluded by Go tooling and go:embed. |
| Family type sync | "deferred fix" | **Mandatory go:generate step + validation** | Reviewer A-N3: the codegen fix exists but wasn't applied; implementation must regenerate. |
| Scheme naming | SchemeDefault | **SchemeCanonical** | Reviewer C-N3: format-name describes the output, not its role. |
| Resolve return contract | Implicit | **Documented rule: cross-provider hosting → []ModelRef; multiple canonicals → ErrAmbiguous** | Reviewer C-N1: explicit semantics. |
| Versioned-variant regex | `<base>-v<MAJOR>.<MINOR>` | **Multi-pattern: `<base>-?[vkmM]?<MAJOR>(.<MINOR>)?`** | Reviewer A-N5: kimi-k2.5, qwen3.5, mimo-v2.5 all need to match. |
| Model constants spec | One sentence | **Full deliverable with naming/collision/tests** | Reviewer A-B6: still open from Round 1. |

## MVP Milestone — Deliverables

(Numbered as in PROPOSAL-2; revised content marked.)

1. **`decompose/data/family_overrides.json`** + **`decompose/data/variant_suffixes.json`** + **`decompose/data/version_patterns.json`** (NEW location). Embedded via `go:embed` for runtime, also read by codegen at build time. The `decompose` directory is a regular package location (not testdata/), so embed and tooling work normally.

2. **`decompose.go`**: same as PROPOSAL-2, plus:
   - Loads JSON via `//go:embed decompose/data/*.json` directive (or `embed.FS`)
   - Versioned-variant rule expanded: matches `kimi-k2.5`, `qwen3.5`, `mimo-v2.5-pro`, `minimax-m2.5`, `minimax-m2.7`
   - `inferFamilyFromID` documented for empty-family fallback (25% of models)

3. **`canonical.go`**: same as PROPOSAL-2 with rename:
   - `SchemeDefault` → `SchemeCanonical` (Reviewer C-N3)
   - All call sites updated

4. **`designation.go`**: same as PROPOSAL-2.

5. **Updated `modelref.go`**: ModelRef now has 6 fields (added ID per Reviewer A-N1):
   ```go
   type ModelRef struct {
       ID        ModelID  // NEW: original API ID for SchemeRaw output
       Provider  Provider
       RawFamily Family
       Family    Family
       Variant   string
       Date      string
   }
   ```
   Updated `Ref()`:
   ```go
   func (m ModelInfo) Ref() ModelRef {
       return ModelRef{
           ID:        m.ID,
           Provider:  m.Provider,
           RawFamily: m.Family,
           Family:    m.NormalizedFamily,
           Variant:   m.NormalizedVariant,
           Date:      m.NormalizedDate,
       }
   }
   ```

5b. **NEW `ModelInfo` fields** (per Reviewer A-N4):
   - `NormalizedFamily Family` — populated by codegen from DecomposeFamily(Family).Family
   - `NormalizedVariant string` — populated by codegen from DecomposeFamily(Family).Variant
   - `NormalizedDate string` — populated by codegen from ExtractDate(ID, ReleaseDate)
   - bestiary.schema.json updated to document these fields
   - SQLite store reads/writes them via the v2→v3 migration columns

6. **`resolve.go`**: same as PROPOSAL-2, plus explicit doc rule (Reviewer C-N1):
   ```go
   // Resolve returns []ModelRef.
   //
   // Disambiguation rule:
   //   - If all matches share the same Canonical (provider, family, variant, date)
   //     across multiple providers (cross-provider hosting), returns the slice with
   //     err == nil. Caller can iterate or filter by provider.
   //   - If matches resolve to multiple distinct Canonicals (e.g., user typed
   //     "claude" matching Opus, Sonnet, Haiku), returns *ErrAmbiguous with
   //     candidate list. Caller should refine input or use --scheme.
   //   - Zero matches: returns ErrNotFound.
   ```

7. **`family.go`** (Family methods): same as PROPOSAL-2.

8. **`models_static_gen.go`** + **NEW `models_constants_gen.go`** (per Reviewer A-B6):
   - **Naming convention**: `Model<Provider><FamilyVariant><Date?>` for the const name. PascalCase from canonical components. Date as `YYYYMMDD` if present (no separators in identifier). The constant value is the raw API ModelID string.
   - **Examples**:
     - `ModelAnthropicClaudeOpus20250514 ModelID = "claude-opus-4-20250514"`
     - `ModelAnthropicClaudeSonnet20250815 ModelID = "claude-sonnet-4-20250815"`
     - `ModelOpenAIGPTCodexMini ModelID = "gpt-codex-mini"` (no date)
     - `ModelGoogleGeminiFlashLitePreview20250417 ModelID = "gemini-2.5-flash-lite-preview-04-17"`
   - **Collision-resolution algorithm**: Two-pass:
     1. Generate naive name `Model<Provider><Family><Variant><Date>`. Track collisions.
     2. For colliding names, append a stable disambiguator. Disambiguators in priority order: (a) version segment from raw ID (e.g., `Model<...>V41` for claude-opus-4-1 vs `Model<...>V40` for claude-opus-4-0), (b) sequential suffix `_<n>` as last resort.
   - **Generated file**: `models_constants_gen.go` (separate from `models_static_gen.go`). Lives in package root, generated by bestiary-gen.
   - **Skip rules**: Models with both empty family AND no extractable Family from ID are NOT given a constant (raw ID still in registry).

9. **CLI extension** (`cmd/bestiary/main.go`): same as PROPOSAL-2.

10. **Cache flags** (`cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go`): same as PROPOSAL-2.

11. **SQLite v2→v3 migration**: same as PROPOSAL-2.

12. **Provenance companion PR**: same as PROPOSAL-2.

13. **`bestiary.schema.json`** updated: includes new ModelInfo fields (NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant, NormalizedDate), Designation array, AcceptabilityRating enum, ModelRef.ID. Schema version `0.0.2`.

14. **Codegen template regeneration** (per Reviewer A-N3): `go generate ./...` MUST be run as part of implementation. Post-condition validation: families_gen.go contains `type Family string` (no `=`). The implementation worker must verify this.

15. **Test additions**: see Test Plan below.

## Public Interfaces (delta from PROPOSAL-2)

### `modelref.go` — ModelRef now has ID

```go
type ModelRef struct {
    ID        ModelID  // raw API ID; supports SchemeRaw output
    Provider  Provider
    RawFamily Family
    Family    Family
    Variant   string
    Date      string
}

func (r ModelRef) Format(scheme CanonicalScheme) string {
    switch scheme {
    case SchemeCanonical:    // was SchemeDefault
        // family/variant@date
    case SchemeHuggingFace:
        // family-variant@date
    case SchemePURL:
        // pkg:huggingface/provider/family-variant@date
    case SchemeRaw:
        return string(r.ID)  // now possible
    }
}
```

### `bestiary.go` — ModelInfo new fields

```go
type ModelInfo struct {
    ID                ModelID
    Provider          Provider
    DisplayName       string
    Family            Family   // raw API family
    NormalizedFamily  Family   // NEW: codegen-baked decomposition
    NormalizedVariant string   // NEW: codegen-baked decomposition
    NormalizedDate    string   // NEW: codegen-baked from ID/ReleaseDate
    // ... existing fields
}
```

### `decompose/data/family_overrides.json`

```json
{
  "claude-opus":   {"family": "claude", "variant": "opus"},
  "claude-sonnet": {"family": "claude", "variant": "sonnet"},
  "claude-haiku":  {"family": "claude", "variant": "haiku"},
  ...
}
```

### `decompose/data/variant_suffixes.json`

```json
{
  "suffixes": [
    "-flash-lite", "-deep-research", "-codex-spark", "-codex-mini",
    "-codex", "-thinking", "-instruct", "-vision", "-embed", "-embedding",
    "-mini", "-pro", "-flash", "-lite", "-turbo", "-base", "-ultra",
    "-large", "-medium", "-small", "-spark", "-nano", "-free",
```

### `decompose/data/version_patterns.json`

```json
{
  "patterns": [
    {"regex": "^(.+)-v(\\d+\\.\\d+)(-.+)?$", "name": "v-prefix"},
    {"regex": "^(.+)-k(\\d+\\.\\d+)$",       "name": "k-prefix (kimi)"},
    {"regex": "^(.+)-m(\\d+\\.\\d+)$",       "name": "m-prefix (minimax)"},
    {"regex": "^(.+?)(\\d+\\.\\d+)$",        "name": "no-prefix (qwen)"}
  ]
}
```

### `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` — Family type assertion

```go
// After regenerating families_gen.go, verify named type emission:
func validateGeneratedFamilyType(path string) error {
    src, err := os.ReadFile(path)
    if err != nil { return err }
    if !bytes.Contains(src, []byte("type Family string")) {
        return fmt.Errorf(
            "families_gen.go does not declare named Family type\n" +
            "  What: expected `type Family string` (named type)\n" +
            "  Why: Family must be a named type to attach methods\n" +
            "  Where: %s\n" +
            "  How to fix: re-run codegen template; verify generator emits named type",
            path)
    }
    if bytes.Contains(src, []byte("type Family = string")) {
        return fmt.Errorf("families_gen.go declares Family as alias; should be named type")
    }
    return nil
}
```

### `models_constants_gen.go` (NEW)

```go
// Code generated by bestiary-gen. DO NOT EDIT.
package bestiary

const (
    ModelAnthropicClaudeOpus20250514     ModelID = "claude-opus-4-20250514"
    ModelAnthropicClaudeOpus41           ModelID = "claude-opus-4-1"
    ModelAnthropicClaudeSonnet20250815   ModelID = "claude-sonnet-4-20250815"
    ModelOpenAIGPT4o20240806             ModelID = "gpt-4o-2024-08-06"
    ModelOpenAIGPTCodexMini              ModelID = "gpt-codex-mini"
    // ... ~3000-4000 model constants total
)

// allModelConstants is the canonical list of all generated model constants.
var allModelConstants = [...]ModelID{
    ModelAnthropicClaudeOpus20250514,
    // ...
}

// Models returns all generated model constants as a defensive copy.
func Models() []ModelID {
    out := make([]ModelID, len(allModelConstants))
    copy(out, allModelConstants[:])
    return out
}
```

## Test Plan (delta from PROPOSAL-2)

### `decompose_test.go` updates

- Embed verification: `decompose/data/*.json` exists and is loadable

### Implementation post-condition test

```go
// TestFamilyType_NamedNotAlias: families_gen.go must declare named type.
// Catches the prior epoch's regression where regen reverted to alias.
func TestFamilyType_NamedNotAlias(t *testing.T) {
    src, err := os.ReadFile("families_gen.go")
    if err != nil { t.Fatal(err) }
    if !bytes.Contains(src, []byte("type Family string")) {
        t.Error("families_gen.go must declare `type Family string` (named type)")
    }
    if bytes.Contains(src, []byte("type Family = string")) {
        t.Error("families_gen.go must not declare Family as alias")
    }
}
```

### `modelref_test.go` updates

- `TestModelRef_HasID`: ModelRef.ID populated for static models
- `TestFormatRaw_UsesID`: Format(SchemeRaw) returns string(r.ID)

(All other tests from PROPOSAL-2 carry forward unchanged.)

## Validation Checklist (delta from PROPOSAL-2)

- [ ] ModelRef has `ID ModelID` field
- [ ] `Format(SchemeRaw)` returns the original API ID
- [ ] `decompose/data/family_overrides.json` exists in regular package directory
- [ ] `decompose/data/variant_suffixes.json` exists
- [ ] `decompose/data/version_patterns.json` exists
- [ ] Files are reachable via `go:embed` (NOT in testdata/)
- [ ] Implementation runs `go generate ./...` and verifies families_gen.go has named type
- [ ] `validateGeneratedFamilyType` post-condition runs in codegen
- [ ] `models_constants_gen.go` exists with ~3000+ constants
- [ ] Naming convention produces unique constants (collision algorithm tested)
- [ ] `SchemeDefault` renamed to `SchemeCanonical` everywhere
- [ ] Versioned-variant patterns match k2.5, qwen3.5, m2.5, v2.5 cases
- [ ] ModelInfo has NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant, NormalizedDate fields
- [ ] bestiary.schema.json documents all new fields
- [ ] Resolve doc explains []ModelRef vs *ErrAmbiguous distinction explicitly
- [ ] All PROPOSAL-2 checklist items still pass

## BDD Acceptance Criteria (delta from PROPOSAL-2)

**Given** raw family "kimi-k2.5" **when** `DecomposeFamily` is called **then** returns `("kimi", "k2.5")` via versioned-variant rule with k-prefix pattern **should not** require v-prefix specifically

**Given** raw family "qwen3.5" **when** `DecomposeFamily` is called **then** returns `("qwen", "3.5")` via no-prefix versioned pattern **should not** be skipped because no separator letter precedes the digits

**Given** raw family "minimax-m2.5" **when** `DecomposeFamily` is called **then** returns `("minimax", "m2.5")` **should not** confuse with the suffix table

**Given** ModelRef{ID:"claude-opus-4-20250514", ...} **when** `Format(SchemeRaw)` is called **then** returns "claude-opus-4-20250514" (the ID) **should not** require ModelInfo lookup

**Given** input "claude" **when** `Resolve` is called **then** returns *ErrAmbiguous because the input matches multiple distinct Canonicals (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku) **should not** return []ModelRef when the canonicals differ

**Given** canonical "claude/opus@2025-05-14" hosted by anthropic AND amazon-bedrock AND azure **when** `Resolve` is called **then** returns []ModelRef of length 3 (cross-provider hosting, same Canonical) **should not** error since the Canonical is unique

**Given** PROPOSAL-3 implementation runs **when** `go generate ./...` completes **then** families_gen.go declares `type Family string` (named type) **should not** declare `type Family = string` (alias)

**Given** `decompose/data/family_overrides.json` **when** the package is compiled **then** the JSON is embedded into the binary via `go:embed` **should not** be excluded by testdata/ rules

**Given** any model in the static registry **when** `Models()` is called **then** the returned slice contains a unique constant pointing to its API ID **should not** have collisions or duplicates

## Why this exists

Wave 3 (UAT-3 fix wave) introduces Family.CanonicalProvider() Provider — a method that tells Resolve which provider should be preferred when canonical-form input matches multiple providers.

Per user direction (UAT-3 Decision 2 catch-all, verbatim):
> "We will need to determine the canonical providers for all families of models in a followup. For now, we can just determine the canonical providers for the most popular models and stub the rest with a placeholder value, with a comment specifically stating that the placeholder will be reviewed and filled in during a followup."

Wave 3 (SLICE-FIX-V2-2) populates Family.CanonicalProvider() for popular families (claude→Anthropic, gemini→Google, gpt→OpenAI, llama→Local, etc.) and leaves a TODO placeholder for the rest. This followup completes coverage.

## Scope of this followup

Determine the canonical provider for the remaining families (~155 families minus the popular ones populated in Wave 3). For each family:
- Identify the originating organization (likely the API provider whose name matches family root, but verify per case)
- Identify cases where there is no clear "canonical" provider (community-trained models, multi-org collabs, etc.) — for these, Family.CanonicalProvider() returns the empty Provider and Resolve falls back to ErrAmbiguous as designed
- Update the static map / method body in Wave 3's implementation
- Remove the "review during followup" placeholder comments

## Acceptance
- All non-trivial families have an explicit CanonicalProvider mapping (or explicit empty-return with rationale comment)
- TODO placeholder comment block referencing this followup (`bestiary-1wy7`) removed from family.go
- Tests cover the populated mappings + empty-return-on-unknown contract
title: FOLLOWUP_SLICE-2: Canonical provider mapping coverage for all families

description:
---
references:
  request: bestiary-n6x
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  elicit: bestiary-73x
  proposal: bestiary-1oq (RATIFIED)
  uat: bestiary-qz7
  prior_proposal: bestiary-4m5 (superseded), bestiary-qk1 (superseded)
---

## Handoff: Architect → Supervisor

### Context
Entity normalization pipeline. The deferred work from the codegen epoch (REQUEST bestiary-u9k → URD bestiary-xek) is now in scope. Goal: canonical representation of model and provider names, allowing users to ask "show me Claude Opus 4 anywhere" and find all 4+ provider expressions of the same weights.

### Ratified Plan: PROPOSAL-3 + 5 UAT Revisions

### Deliverables Summary

1. **`parse/data/family_overrides.json`**, **`parse/data/variant_suffixes.json`**, **`parse/data/version_patterns.json`** — embedded JSON data for parsing rules.

2. **`parse.go`** (NEW) — `ParseFamily(raw Family) (Family, string)`, `ExtractDate(id ModelID, releaseDate string) string`, `inferFamilyFromID(id ModelID, p Provider) Family`. NOTE: full rename from "decompose" terminology.

3. **`canonical.go`** (NEW) — `CanonicalScheme` int enum: SchemeCanonical, SchemeHuggingFace, SchemePURL, SchemeRaw. Format definitions per UAT Option A:
   - SchemeCanonical: `<provider>/<family>/<variant>@<date>` (e.g., `anthropic/claude/opus@2025-05-14`) OR a provider's actual representation
   - SchemeHuggingFace: `<provider>/<raw-id>` matching real HF Hub (e.g., `anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514`)
   - SchemePURL: `pkg:huggingface/<provider>/<raw-id>` (per ECMA-427:2025 + purl-spec PR #201)
   - SchemeRaw: original API ID (e.g., `claude-opus-4-20250514`)

4. **`designation.go`** (NEW) — `Designation{Value, Scheme, Provider, Rating}`, `AcceptabilityRating` int enum (Admitted, Preferred, Deprecated). All static designations default to Admitted.

5. **Updated `modelref.go`** — ModelRef has 6 fields including new `ID ModelID`. Methods: String() (Stringer using SchemeCanonical), Format(scheme), Designations(). Drop Canonical() projector method (Canonical type itself was already dropped in PROPOSAL-2).

6. **Updated `bestiary.go` ModelInfo** — adds `NormalizedFamily Family`, `NormalizedVariant string`, `NormalizedDate string` (codegen-baked).

7. **`resolve.go`** (NEW) — `Resolve(input string, opts ...ResolveOption) ([]ModelRef, error)`. Returns slice for cross-provider hosting (same canonical, different providers). Returns *ErrAmbiguous when input matches multiple distinct canonicals. Returns ErrNotFound for zero matches. WithScheme(s) option pins scheme.

8. **`family.go`** (NEW) — Family.IsKnown(), String(), MarshalText(), UnmarshalText(). Resolves bestiary-ptm follow-up debt. Requires families_gen.go to have `type Family string` (named type), NOT `type Family = string` (alias) — implementation MUST run `go generate ./...` to apply the codegen template fix from commit 6a8d75b.

9. **`models_constants_gen.go`** (NEW, generated) — Per-canonical model ID constants with underscore-separated naming:
   - Format: `Model_<Provider>_<Family>_<Variant>_<Version>?_<Date>?` (only Model constants use underscores; Provider/Family/Harness keep PascalCase — no break)
   - Examples: `Model_Anthropic_Claude_Opus_4_20250514`, `Model_OpenAI_GPT_4o_20240806`, `Model_Google_Gemini_2_5_Flash_Lite_Preview_20250417`
   - Naming derived from parser output components, joined by `_`
   - Within-component characters preserved (e.g., `4o` stays `4o`, not `4_o`)
   - Two-pass collision resolution: append disambiguator from raw ID version segment, fall back to `_<n>` sequential suffix
   - Skip rule: empty family AND no extractable family from ID → no constant generated
   - `Models() []ModelID` returns all generated constants

10. **CLI extension `cmd/bestiary/main.go`** — `bestiary show <input> [--scheme=<canonical|huggingface|purl|raw>]`. Auto-detects scheme by prefix. ErrAmbiguous prints candidate list.

11. **Cache flags `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go`** — `--cache-dir <path>` and `--no-fetch`. Cache miss with `--no-fetch` → actionable error (path, reason, remediation).

12. **SQLite v2→v3 migration `store.go`** — column renames per UAT:
    - Existing `family` column RENAMED to `raw_family`
    - NEW columns: `family` (parsed), `variant`, `date`
    - NEW index: `idx_canonical ON models(family, variant, provider)`
    - Backfill on first migrate: re-runs parse logic on existing rows
    - NEW Store method `QueryByCanonical(family, variant, date) ([]ModelInfo, error)` exercises the index
    - Plus a separate SQL migration file (testdata/migrations/v2_to_v3.sql or migrations/v2_to_v3.sql)

13. **Provenance companion PR** (separate repo `dayvidpham/provenance`):
    - `provenance.Provider.IsValid()` delegates to `bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown()`
    - `provenance.Provider.MarshalText()` becomes permissive (any value round-trips)
    - `provenance.Provider.UnmarshalText()` becomes permissive (accepts any string)
    - Adapter `RegistryFromBestiary` retained for ModelInfo conversion
    - Tests updated: remove 4-value assumption, add 110-provider validation
    - go.mod bump to bestiary v0.0.2 once released

14. **`bestiary.schema.json`** — updated to document NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date, ModelRef.ID, Designation array, AcceptabilityRating enum. Schema version `0.0.2`.

15. **Codegen template regeneration** — implementation MUST run `go generate ./...` and verify families_gen.go declares `type Family string` (no `=`). PROPOSAL-3's `validateGeneratedFamilyType` function in codegen guards this post-condition.

16. **Migration documentation**:
    - `MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md` — single document covering: new ModelInfo fields, ModelRef shape change (ID added), parse package introduction, SQL column renames, Model_ constants naming convention, schema version bump
    - SQL migration file: `migrations/v2_to_v3.sql` (or similar location) containing the ALTER TABLE / CREATE INDEX statements

### Key Decisions

1. **No LRM types** — simple canonical triple is the goal (Family, Variant, Date), Provider is metadata.
2. **Parse, not decompose** — terminology rename per UAT.
3. **Suffix table + manual overrides** — deterministic, auditable. Splink overkill at 4,168 records.
4. **Tuple as canonical, multi-format strings** — Format(scheme) dispatch with typed enum (SchemeCanonical default).
5. **All Designations are AcceptabilityAdmitted** — promotion to Preferred deferred to curation epoch.
6. **Bake decomposition into static gen** — runtime Ref() is pure projection. Zero allocation/runtime parsing for the common case.
7. **Embed via decompose/data/ → parse/data/** — JSON files in regular package directory (NOT testdata/), embedded via go:embed for runtime.
8. **Resolve returns []ModelRef + *ErrAmbiguous** — slice for cross-provider hosting (same canonical), error for distinct canonicals.
9. **Provenance: keep type, delegate validation** — provenance.Provider stays; IsValid/Marshal/Unmarshal all become permissive aligned with bestiary's model.
10. **Schema version 0.0.1 → 0.0.2** (NOT 0.1.0) — additive changes, patch bump per user correction.
11. **Model constants ONLY use underscores** — Provider/Family/Harness keep PascalCase (no breaking change). Migration doc covers Model_* additions only.

### Open Items
- Curation review for AcceptabilityPreferred promotion — future epoch
- Full SBOM/PURL ecosystem integration (CycloneDX, OSV) — future epoch
- "Deprecated" rating assignments — future epoch
- Bidirectional HuggingFace Hub mapping — future epoch

### Acceptance Criteria

(See PROPOSAL-3 BDD section for full set; updated per UAT)

- ParseFamily handles 169 raw families + 30+ suffixes + override table + versioned variants (dotted: kimi-k2.5; hyphenated: claude-opus-4-5)
- ExtractDate matches only full YYYYMMDD or YYYY-MM-DD
- Resolve returns []ModelRef for cross-provider hosting, *ErrAmbiguous for multiple canonicals
- ModelRef has 6 fields including ID
- Format(SchemeRaw) returns the original API ID
- Format(SchemeHuggingFace) returns provider/raw-id format
- Format(SchemePURL) returns pkg:huggingface/provider/raw-id
- bestiary show accepts --scheme flag with auto-detect default
- bestiary-gen --no-fetch + cache miss → actionable error
- SQLite v2→v3 migration: 5 sub-tests pass (preserves-data, backfill, idempotent, edge-cases, index-used)
- Model constants follow Model_<Provider>_<Family>_<Variant>_<Date> underscore convention
- Provider/Family/Harness PascalCase preserved
- families_gen.go declares `type Family string` (named type)
- BestiarySchemaVersion exact string "0.0.2"
- bestiary.schema.json conformance test passes
- MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md exists with all renames documented
- SQL migration file exists
- go vet ./... + CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./... pass

### Suggested Slice Decomposition

- **Slice 1**: parse package (parse.go, parse/data/*.json, ParseFamily/ExtractDate, tests). Foundation for everything else.
- **Slice 2**: ModelInfo extension + family methods + family.go. Bake parsed fields into static gen via codegen change.
- **Slice 3**: ModelRef + canonical.go + designation.go + Resolve + resolve.go. Public API.
- **Slice 4**: Model_ constants generation. Depends on parse + codegen baked fields.
- **Slice 5**: SQLite v2→v3 migration + QueryByCanonical + SQL migration file. Schema migration.
- **Slice 6**: CLI extension (`bestiary show` with --scheme) + cache flags (`bestiary-gen --cache-dir, --no-fetch`).
- **Slice 7**: bestiary.schema.json update + version bump + MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md.
- **Slice 8**: Provenance companion PR (separate repo `dayvidpham/provenance`).

But supervisor decides final decomposition based on dependency analysis.
title: HANDOFF: Architect → Supervisor for entity normalization

description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-hf4
  reviewer: reviewer-C
  round: 1
---
## What went wrong
Commit 2a0b715 added three schema properties (NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant, NormalizedDate) and three required-array entries to bestiary.schema.json — a file explicitly excluded from this slice's scope.

## Why it matters
The bestiary-hf4 spec says: 'bestiary.schema.json — NOT this slice (Slice 7 owns schema doc)'. Slice 7 (bestiary-0ug0) owns the canonical schema edit, the BestiarySchemaVersion bump to 0.0.2, and the migration document. By pre-editing the schema here:
1. Slice 7 faces a merge/conflict risk since the slice-7 worker expects to write the same properties.
2. BestiarySchemaVersion was NOT bumped in version.go (still 0.0.1), so the schema.json content is inconsistent with the version advertised to callers.
3. The CLAUDE.md rule says: 'When modifying public types — Increment BestiarySchemaVersion in version.go'. ModelInfo gained 3 new public fields but version.go was untouched.

## Where
- File: bestiary.schema.json (lines 25-37, 164-166), commit 2a0b715
- File: version.go (BestiarySchemaVersion = "0.0.1" — not bumped)

## How to fix
Revert the bestiary.schema.json changes made in commit 2a0b715. Leave the schema.json in its pre-hf4 state. Slice 7 will perform the canonical update (properties + version bump + migration doc) as specified. If the conformance test (TestJSONOutput_ConformsToSchema) currently passes it does so against the already-edited schema — reverting will cause schema mismatch that Slice 7 is responsible for resolving.
title: BLOCKER: Slice 2b edited bestiary.schema.json despite spec saying NOT this slice

## Specification
Two orthogonal CLI changes bundled in one slice (single owner of cmd/* paths to avoid contention):

A. `bestiary show <input> [--scheme=<canonical|huggingface|purl|raw>]` — auto-detects scheme by prefix when flag absent. Resolves via Resolve(). Prints candidate table on *ErrAmbiguous.

B. `bestiary-gen [--cache-dir <path>] [--no-fetch]` — overrides hardcoded `.bestiary-gen-cache/`; `--no-fetch` reads cached api_response.json instead of HTTP. Cache miss with --no-fetch → actionable error per C-actionable-errors.

## Files Owned
- cmd/bestiary/main.go (add --scheme flag to show subcommand; replace direct LookupModel call with Resolve; render *ErrAmbiguous candidate table)
- cmd/bestiary/main_test.go (scheme tests; ambiguity rendering test)
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go (parseFlags additions; cache path threading; --no-fetch read-cache path)
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go (cache flag tests)
- format.go (helper for rendering ErrAmbiguous candidate table — reuse tableRow pattern)

## Auto-detect rules
| Input prefix | Scheme |
|---|---|
| `pkg:huggingface/` | SchemePURL |
| Contains `/` (and no `pkg:`) | SchemeHuggingFace |
| Default | SchemeCanonical (which falls through to raw lookup if no canonical match) |
| Last-resort fall-through | SchemeRaw — direct ModelID lookup |

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] `bestiary show claude-opus-4-20250514` returns the model.
- [ ] `bestiary show "anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514"` (HuggingFace form) resolves.
- [ ] `bestiary show "pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514"` (PURL) resolves.
- [ ] `bestiary show claude --scheme=canonical` returns *ErrAmbiguous with Opus/Sonnet/Haiku candidates listed.
- [ ] `bestiary-gen --cache-dir /tmp/foo` writes to /tmp/foo/api_response.json.
- [ ] `bestiary-gen --no-fetch` with stale cache succeeds; with missing cache returns actionable error containing path + remediation.

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)
- Given input "claude" When `bestiary show claude` Then exit code != 0 and stdout/stderr lists candidates from *ErrAmbiguous.
- Given input "pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514" When `bestiary show` Then output is the resolved model.
- Given a missing cache file When `bestiary-gen --no-fetch --cache-dir /nonexistent` Then exit != 0 and message tells the user (a) what file was expected, (b) why fetch was skipped (--no-fetch), (c) how to fix (drop --no-fetch or place a cached api_response.json at the path).

## Owner Worker
Wave 4, model: sonnet.
title: SLICE-6a: bestiary show --scheme + ErrAmbiguous rendering

close_reason: Fixed in commit bb0ef2b: add time.Parse round-trip validation in extractDateFromString; add TestExtractDate_CalendarValidation
description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-n2q
  reviewer: reviewer-A
  round: 1
  severity_group: bestiary-czu0
---
## Finding

ExtractDate uses two regexes to match YYYYMMDD and YYYY-MM-DD patterns. The patterns correctly match the structural form but do not validate that month (MM) is 01-12 or day (DD) is 01-31. A model ID like 'model-9999-99-99' returns '9999-99-99' without error or truncation.

## Impact

Low in this epoch: real model IDs from models.dev use valid dates. This could produce nonsensical output if the upstream API ever returns malformed date strings.

## Suggestion

Add a parse-time validation step in extractDateFromString to verify MM ∈ [01,12] and DD ∈ [01,31]. Or document explicitly that date validation is out-of-scope for this epoch (acceptable for follow-up). This is not a blocker but should be tracked.
title: IMPORTANT: ExtractDate accepts semantically invalid dates (e.g., 9999-99-99)

description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ektc
  reviewer: B (Test Quality)
  round: 2
  file: cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go
  lines: 350-361
---
## Finding

TestNoFetch_HitsCache now wires apiURL = srv.URL (correctly fixing the cycle-1 IMPORTANT bestiary-szxm). However, when fetchModelsWithRaw is called with noFetch=true, the noFetch branch reads from disk via os.ReadFile and returns before ever reaching the 'http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, apiURL, ...)' call. Therefore apiURL is never read in this code path and contacted can never be set to true regardless of what apiURL is overridden to.

The guard is structurally sound: if a future refactor removes the noFetch guard and makes an HTTP call, apiURL=srv.URL does ensure it would hit the guard server. But the current test narrative ('any accidental HTTP fetch would hit our guard server') is slightly aspirational — it only works if the regression is of the form 'calls apiURL directly'. A regression that uses a hardcoded URL or a separate http.Get would bypass it.

This is a documentation gap more than a code gap. The test is correct and the contacted=false assertion is valid.

## How to fix (optional follow-up)

A comment clarifying the residual limitation would be sufficient:
  // Note: contacted trips only if a regression calls apiURL. A hardcoded URL regression would not be caught.

Not required for acceptance.
title: MINOR: contacted flag in TestNoFetch_HitsCache cannot trip via apiURL override on noFetch=true path

## Components Demonstrated (Wave 3 fix verification + new Modifier feature)

### Component 1: JSON field rename (drop Normalized prefix)
Demonstrated: `bestiary show --format raw claude-opus-4-5-20251101` →
RawFamily / Family / Variant / Version / Date / Modifier (no "Normalized" prefix).
Q (RawFamily exposure): "Keep RawFamily always (current)"

### Component 3: Resolve overhaul
Demonstrated:
- canonical-provider preference: `claude/opus@2025-11-24` → Provider=anthropic (not rehost)
- bare-family ambiguous: grouped by (Family,Variant,Version) + truncated at 10 with "+N more"
- `--format` default=peasant (no auto-detect); `--format purl pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5` returns anthropic
- PURL loose-fallback works functionally but the "no matches in namespace X" note is on ErrAmbiguous.Error() (errors.go:100-103) and NOT printed by FormatAmbiguous (format.go:321)

Q1 (canonical-provider pref scope in ambiguous list): "Prefer canonical provider in ambiguous list too"
Q2 (missed-namespace note): "Fix now before accepting C3"
Q-feedback (verbatim): "In Demo B, we should disambiguate between the actual canonical provider anthropic and any rehosts"
Decision: **REVISE**

Required fixes (Wave 4, C3-only):
1. In FormatAmbiguous (and/or Resolve candidate ordering), surface the canonical provider's rows (Family.CanonicalProvider()) and VISUALLY DISTINGUISH them from rehosts in the ambiguous table. Bare-family `claude` should show anthropic's canonical rows, marked as canonical, separate from rehost rows.
2. Wire FormatAmbiguous to print the PURL loose-fallback note ("no matches in namespace %q — performing loose match across all providers") that currently only exists on ErrAmbiguous.Error().

### Component 5: Parse-failure audit log
Demonstrated: `.bestiary-gen-cache/parse_failures.json` — schema_version, generated_at, failure_count=121, full-detail records (raw_id/provider/raw_family/attempted_parse/reason).
Q (audit intent): "Yes — file + full detail is what I wanted"
Decision: **ACCEPT**

### Component 6: Provenance Provider.IsValid restoration
Demonstrated: provenance/pkg/ptypes/enums.go imports bestiary; `func (p Provider) IsValid() bool { return bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown() }`. Redundant adapter funcs removed; dual-API finding bestiary-n7hh resolved.
Q (case policy): "Case-sensitive (current)"
Decision: **ACCEPT**

### New Feature: Modifier field (8-field ModelRef + bracket-suffix canonical + constant slot)
Demonstrated:
- happy path: `kimi-k2-thinking` → `alibaba-cn/kimi@2025-11-06[thinking]` (bracket suffix renders)
- 208 models populated (thinking/preview/latest/code/vision/think)
- broken: empty-RawFamily rehost `claude-opus-4-6-thinking` (302ai) → Family="claude-opus-4-6", Variant="thinking", Version="", Modifier="" (4 fields wrong); InferFamilyFromID path bypasses ExtractModifier+version extraction
Q (empty-RawFamily handling): "FOLLOWUP, accept Modifier now (recommended)" → tracked as bestiary-4yuy
Decision: **ACCEPT**

## Final Decision
**REVISE — only Component 3 needs fixes (2 items above).** Components 1, 5, 6, and the new Modifier feature ACCEPTED. Drives a focused Wave 4 (C3-only) fix, then UAT-5.

## New findings logged
- PURL loose-fallback diagnostic not surfaced via CLI (now part of C3 REVISE, fix-now)
- GitHub issue #3 created documenting full epoch status + diagnostic findings
title: UAT-4: Implementation acceptance for entity normalization (Wave 3 demo)
