close_reason: Already resolved by provenance commit 69644ac. Option C from the finding was implemented: provenance.IsKnown(p) in adapter.go:8-20 delegates to bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown(). pkg/ptypes.Provider.IsValid() remains non-empty check (documented), while catalog-membership is available via IsKnown(). All doc comments in enums.go:300-345 explicitly redirect callers to IsKnown() for catalog membership. Deviation ratified. Verified at HEAD of ~/codebases/dayvidpham/provenance/adapter.go and pkg/ptypes/enums.go.
description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-8yfq
  reviewer: Reviewer-C
  round: 1
---
## Finding

The spec (SLICE-8-L1, bestiary-xdej) says:

> Provider.IsValid() — delegate to bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown()

The worker instead implemented:

> IsValid returns true for any non-empty, non-whitespace-only string

This is a deliberate deviation. The worker's rationale (from the commit message and beads comment) is that pkg/ptypes must remain zero-dep and importing bestiary would create a circular/layering violation.

## Why this is IMPORTANT, not BLOCKER

The deviation is architecturally defensible — the zero-dep constraint for pkg/ptypes is a genuine design property of the provenance repo, and the IsKnown delegation would require importing bestiary into the leaf package. The worker documented the reasoning clearly and explicitly annotated every doc comment with the redirect ('use bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown() at the call-site when catalog membership must be enforced'). Tests pass, contracts are honest.

However: this is a unilateral spec change — the spec literally said 'delegate to bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown()'. The architect wrote that spec. The worker changed the contract without going back to the architect/supervisor for ratification. The new IsValid semantics (non-empty check) are weaker than specified (catalog membership check).

## Risk

Callers who call provenance.Provider.IsValid() expecting IsKnown-level semantics will silently accept non-catalog strings. This is the exact risk the spec tried to prevent. The doc comment mitigates it by pointing to IsKnown, but a caller who skims the method name ('IsValid') without reading the doc will still get the wrong mental model.

## Recommendation

The worker should surface this as an explicit architectural decision to the supervisor/architect, with the following options:

Option A (ratify the deviation): Accept IsValid = non-empty check; add a bestiary.Provider.IsKnown()-delegating helper at the ROOT provenance package level (adapter.go or a new provider_validation.go) so that callers who need catalog membership have a provenance-native API without reaching into pkg/ptypes. The spec's intent (easy catalog membership check) is still served.

Option B (fulfill spec, break zero-dep constraint): Import bestiary in pkg/ptypes. This is a layering regression but makes the API match the spec literally.

Option C (add adapter-level wrapper): Keep IsValid as non-empty check in pkg/ptypes; add provenance.IsKnownProvider(p ptypes.Provider) bool that delegates to bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown() in the root package. This is cleaner than Option A and preserves layering.

Option C is the cleanest path. The root package (provenance) already imports bestiary (adapter.go line 4), so a thin wrapper there costs nothing and gives callers the catalog-membership API they need.
title: IMPORTANT: IsValid deviation from spec needs explicit ratification
SLICE-FIX-V4-1-REVIEW-C-1 IMPORTANT

description:
---
references:
  followup_epic: bestiary-602y
  origin_slice: bestiary-12tn
  origin_uat: bestiary-43ib
  related_mapping_fill: bestiary-1wy7
  review_finding: C2-MINOR-2 (axis C, cycle-2 re-review)
---
## Finding (USER-ELEVATED to IMPORTANT, 2026-05-30)
`bestiary show minimax` lists a provider literally named "minimax" inside the
"Also rehosted by:" section:

  Also rehosted by:
    minimax            <- self-referential / actually the canonical source
    deepinfra
    ollama-cloud
    alibaba-cn
    minimax-cn-coding-plan
  +40 more

## Why it happens
The `minimax` family has NO entry in Family.CanonicalProvider() (returns "" — one of
~45 unmapped families, see family.go default case). So FormatAmbiguous cannot recognize
the `minimax` provider as canonical: Section 1 (Canonical) is suppressed (empty), and the
canonical source is dumped into the rehost list alongside true rehosts. The user sees the
familys own originating provider mislabeled as a "rehost".

## Why it matters (user: "important")
Defeats the canonical-vs-rehost disambiguation for any family whose canonical provider is
unmapped. The provider that should head the Canonical section appears as a rehost — the
opposite of the intended signal.

## Fix direction (FOLLOWUP, NOT this slice)
Primary: fill the canonical-provider mapping for self-named / well-known families in
Family.CanonicalProvider() (this is exactly bestiary-1wy7s scope) — e.g. minimax->minimax,
and the other ~45 unmapped families. Once mapped, minimax surfaces in the Canonical section
(with `*`) and is excluded from rehosts by collectRehostProviders.
Secondary (defensive, optional): when CanonicalProvider()=="" but a provider name equals the
family name, treat it as the canonical source heuristically — discuss before implementing
(heuristics can misfire; static mapping is preferred per project standards).

## Acceptance (when addressed in FOLLOWUP)
`bestiary show minimax` shows the minimax provider in a Canonical section (marked `*`),
not in the rehost list.
title: FOLLOWUP(important): self-referential provider in rehost list when family lacks CanonicalProvider mapping (e.g. minimax)

description:
---
references:
  impl_plan: bestiary-pe7
  urd: bestiary-0pi

---
## Specification

Foundation layer: Go module init, all core types, and their methods. Every other slice depends on this.

## Files Owned
- go.mod (module github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary, go 1.24, require zombiezen.com/go/sqlite)
- go.sum (generated)
- bestiary.go (package doc, ModelID, ModelInfo)
- provider.go (Provider type, constants, IsKnown, String, MarshalText, UnmarshalText)
- modality.go (Modality enum, Modalities struct, String with bounds check, MarshalText, UnmarshalText)
- errors.go (ErrNotFound, ErrAPIUnavailable with Error() methods)

## Type Contracts (from PROPOSAL-2)

### ModelID (bestiary.go)
type ModelID string

### ModelInfo (bestiary.go)
type ModelInfo struct {
    ID                    ModelID
    Provider              Provider
    DisplayName           string
    Family                string
    ContextWindow         int
    MaxOutput             int
    Reasoning             bool
    ToolCall              bool
    Attachment            bool
    Temperature           bool
    StructuredOutput      bool
    Interleaved           bool
    OpenWeights           bool
    CostInputPerMTok      *float64
    CostOutputPerMTok     *float64
    CostReasoningPerMTok  *float64
    CostCacheReadPerMTok  *float64
    CostCacheWritePerMTok *float64
    ReleaseDate           string
    Knowledge             string
    Modalities            Modalities
    LastSynced            string   // RFC3339
}

### Provider (provider.go)
type Provider string
const (
    ProviderAnthropic Provider = "anthropic"
    ProviderGoogle    Provider = "google"
    ProviderOpenAI    Provider = "openai"
    ProviderLocal     Provider = "local"
)
func (p Provider) IsKnown() bool — returns true for the 4 constants
func (p Provider) String() string
func (p Provider) MarshalText() ([]byte, error)
func (p *Provider) UnmarshalText(b []byte) error

### Modality (modality.go)
type Modality int
const (
    ModalityText  Modality = iota
    ModalityImage
    ModalityPDF
    ModalityAudio
    ModalityVideo
)
func (m Modality) String() string — MUST bounds-check: if m < 0 || int(m) >= len(modalityNames) return fmt.Sprintf("Modality(%d)", m)
type Modalities struct { Input []Modality; Output []Modality }
MarshalText/UnmarshalText for Modality

### Errors (errors.go)
type ErrNotFound struct { What, Key string } — func (e *ErrNotFound) Error() string
type ErrAPIUnavailable struct { URL string; Attempts int; Cause error } — func (e *ErrAPIUnavailable) Error() string; func (e *ErrAPIUnavailable) Unwrap() error
Use errors.As (not errors.Is) for these structured types.

## Source Material
Adapt from ~/dev/agent-data-leverage/develop/internal/ingest/store_iface.go:138-154 for ModelInfo struct.
Helper functions pattern: ~/dev/agent-data-leverage/develop/internal/store/models_data.go (strPtr, intPtr, floatPtr)

## Acceptance Criteria
Given bestiary package imported When accessing Provider constants Then ProviderAnthropic == "anthropic", etc.
Given Modality(99) When calling String() Then returns "Modality(99)" not panic
Given ErrNotFound{What: "model", Key: "foo"} When using errors.As Then matches *ErrNotFound

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] go.mod declares module github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary with go 1.24
- [ ] All 17 ModelInfo fields match proposal exactly
- [ ] Provider.IsKnown() true for 4 constants, false for arbitrary strings
- [ ] Modality.String() bounds-checks (no panic on out-of-range)
- [ ] MarshalText/UnmarshalText round-trip for Provider and Modality
- [ ] Error types implement error interface with actionable messages
- [ ] ErrAPIUnavailable implements Unwrap() for errors.As chain
- [ ] go vet ./... passes

## Finding

PROPOSAL-3 spec (bestiary-2u5) and PROPOSAL-3 deliverable text describe:
`TestModelConstants_NamingStable: re-run codegen via 'go test -tags=codegen-stability' or use a recorded fixture; assert deterministic naming.`

Worker's implementation tests:
1. Defensive copy (mutation of returned slice does not affect next call)
2. Values do not start with "Model_" (they're raw API IDs)

The actual DETERMINISM check (re-run codegen twice and compare constant names) is NOT implemented.

## Impact

Low: The codegen IS deterministic (uses `sort.Slice` by constant name in `generateConstantsSource`), and the sort is verified transitively by `TestGenerateConstantsSource_Compiles`. The determinism test would be an integration-level test requiring network or a large fixture, which explains the worker's omission.

The test name "NamingStable" implies a property the body doesn't actually exercise.

## Suggestion

Either rename the test to reflect what it actually tests (e.g. `TestModelIDs_Values`) and create a separate `TestModelConstants_Deterministic` that calls `generateConstantsSource` twice with the same input and asserts identical output, OR add a note in the test doc comment explaining why determinism is guaranteed statically (sort.Slice) and not tested at runtime.
title: MINOR: TestModelConstants_NamingStable does not test determinism as spec requires

description:
---
references:
  fix_plan: bestiary-sj74
  uat: bestiary-ozew
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  proposal: bestiary-1oq
  slice_fix_1: bestiary-gkpx
  urd_requirements: [R2, R3]
---
## Specification (Component 3)

UAT-2 Component 3 REVISE: 3 Resolve bugs + 1 data-consistency concern. Depends on SLICE-FIX-1 (bestiary-gkpx) for the Version field shape.

## Bugs

1. **Canonical-form auto-detect broken** (`resolve.go:150-172`): `Resolve('claude/opus@2025-11-01')` returns ErrNotFound. `detectScheme()` has no SchemeCanonical branch — it defaults to SchemeRaw and tries an exact ID match against the static registry. Add detection: input has 1-3 `/` separators and either `@`-date OR a versioned token → infer SchemeCanonical.
2. **PURL provider filter ignored** (`resolve.go:151-158, 203-211`): `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5')` returns 7 refs but ZERO from Anthropic. `detectScheme` strips `pkg:huggingface/anthropic/` segments and passes only the bare ID forward. The provider segment must be retained as a filter hint and applied during/after matching.
3. **Bare-family ErrNotFound instead of ErrAmbiguous** (`resolve.go:170-172, 232-234`): `Resolve('claude')` returns ErrNotFound. SchemeRaw default does exact-ID match on the literal `claude` (no model has that exact ID). Should fall back to SchemeCanonical and group by Family — return *ErrAmbiguous with a candidate list when multiple Family entries match.
4. **Inconsistent decompositions for same raw ID across providers**: e.g.,
   - Anthropic `claude-opus-4-5`, raw_family=`claude-opus` → ParseFamily → `(claude, opus)`
   - QiHang-AI `claude-opus-4-5-20251101`, raw_family=`claude-opus` → ParseFamily → `(claude, opus)` ✓
   - Nano-GPT `claude-opus-4-5-20251101`, raw_family=`""` → InferFamilyFromID → `(claude, "")` ✗
   `InferFamilyFromID` (parse.go:347-361) extracts only family with NO variant. Fix: have InferFamilyFromID extract variant (and version, post SLICE-FIX-1) the same way ParseFamily does, OR have `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go:616-674` re-run ParseFamily on the inferred family + ID tail. The post-SLICE-FIX-1 codegen MUST produce identical (NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant, NormalizedVersion) for the same raw ID across providers.

## Files Owned

- `resolve.go` — fix detectScheme (add canonical branch), add provider hint plumbing in resolveConfig + matching loop, add bare-family canonical fallback that surfaces ErrAmbiguous
- `parse.go` — fix InferFamilyFromID: after extracting family from ID, run the same suffix-stripping / pattern-matching logic to extract variant (and version, post SLICE-FIX-1)
- `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` (genToModelInfo, lines 616-674) — ensure variant + version extraction is consistent regardless of whether raw_family is empty
- `models_static_gen.go` — regenerate via `go generate ./...` after parser fix
- Tests: `resolve_test.go`, `parse_test.go`, `cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go`

## Behaviors

- B1: detectScheme(\"claude/opus@2025-11-01\") returns SchemeCanonical, normalized input.
- B2: detectScheme(\"anthropic/claude/opus/4.5@2025-11-01\") returns SchemeCanonical with provider hint \"anthropic\".
- B3: Resolve(\"pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5\") returns ONLY refs where Provider == anthropic should never include other providers.
- B4: Resolve(\"claude\") returns *ErrAmbiguous with candidate list should never return ErrNotFound.
- B5: For each raw model ID present under multiple providers in the static dataset, the (NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant, NormalizedVersion) decomposition is identical across providers. Codegen-time validation: extend or add a validateGen post-condition.

## Dependencies

- **Depends on SLICE-FIX-1** (bestiary-gkpx) for the Version field shape used in canonical-form auto-detect (B2) and consistency check (B5). DO NOT START until SLICE-FIX-1 has review-passed.

## Integration Points

- Consumes IP-1 (7-field ModelRef from SLICE-FIX-1).
- Consumes IP-3 (parse.go version extraction signature from SLICE-FIX-1).
- Owns no new exports — purely fixes internal Resolve and parse behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Given Resolve(\"claude/opus@2025-11-01\"), when called, then return non-empty refs (or ErrAmbiguous when multi-provider) should never return ErrNotFound.
- Given Resolve(\"pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5\"), when called, then return only Anthropic refs should never include other providers.
- Given Resolve(\"claude\"), when called, then return *ErrAmbiguous with candidate list should never return ErrNotFound.
- Given the static dataset, when iterated, then every (raw_id) present under multiple providers has identical (NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant, NormalizedVersion) should never have decomposition divergence.
- Given CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./... and go vet ./..., when run, then all pass.

## Leaf Tasks
- SLICE-FIX-2-L1: Types (provider hint plumbing in resolveConfig + ResolveOption surface; consistency-check function signature)
- SLICE-FIX-2-L2: Tests (4 failing tests one per bug + cross-provider consistency property test)
- SLICE-FIX-2-L3: Impl (detectScheme canonical branch, PURL provider filter, bare-family fallback to ErrAmbiguous, InferFamilyFromID variant+version extraction, regen)
title: SLICE-FIX-2: Resolve bugs (canonical autodetect, PURL provider filter, bare-family ambiguity, decomposition consistency)

## Finding

TestMigration_V2toV3_PreservesData checks that both rows survive migration and that
m.Family (raw_family) is preserved. It does NOT assert that NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant,
and NormalizedDate were backfilled for those rows.

This means PreservesData only tests structural survival (row count, raw_family identity),
not semantic correctness (canonical fields populated). The Backfill test covers this for
a single row, but PreservesData would be more complete if it also verified canonical fields
for the rows it inserts — since it uses model IDs like 'claude-opus-4-20250514' which have
deterministic parse outputs.

## Suggested fix

Add assertions for NormalizedFamily='claude', NormalizedVariant='opus', NormalizedDate='2025-05-14'
for the claude-opus-4-20250514 row in PreservesData, and NormalizedFamily='gemini-pro' (or whatever
ParseFamily('gemini-pro') returns), NormalizedVariant, NormalizedDate for the gemini-pro row.
title: MINOR: PreservesData test does not assert NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date after migration

description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-hf4
  reviewer: reviewer-B
  round: 1
  severity_group: bestiary-oju4
---
## Finding

TestCacheDirFlag exercises the full run() code path end-to-end (real HTTP mock → codegen → file write), but the test only asserts that api_response.json was written to the correct directory. It never reads back models_static_gen.go and checks that NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date are non-zero for any model.

Likewise, TestNoFetch_HitsCache verifies that the cache is read and models/provMeta are returned, but makes no assertions on the content of the generated file.

The Worker's commit message claims '3 sample models work' and the lm1 task comment has a manual spot-check, but there is no automated assertion proving that:
- claude-opus-4-20250514 → NormalizedFamily='claude', NormalizedVariant='opus', NormalizedDate='2025-05-14'
- gpt-4o-2024-08-06 → NormalizedFamily='gpt', NormalizedVariant='', NormalizedDate='2024-08-06'

This is the core splice path. If someone breaks genToModelInfo or the template in generateSource, the only guard currently is that go build still compiles. The regression would be silent.

## Why IMPORTANT (not BLOCKER)
Tests pass; the code is correct as of now. The risk is future regressions go undetected.

## Suggested fix
Extend TestCacheDirFlag or add a new TestGenToModelInfo_NormalizedFields test that:
1. Constructs a minimalAPIJSON with known family/ID fields.
2. Calls run() or genToModelInfo() directly.
3. Asserts NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date on the returned ModelInfo (or in the written file content).
title: IMPORTANT: no integration test asserting normalized fields on generated models (end-to-end splice path untested)

close_reason: Fixed in commit 908f277: formatCanonical() uses named-type comparison r.Family == '' instead of string(r.Family) == ''
description:
MINOR finding. Slice: bestiary-99m. Reviewer C round 1. Severity group: bestiary-35qs.

modelref.go line 70: 'if string(r.Family) == ""' converts Family (a named string type) to string before comparing. The idiomatic Go form is 'if r.Family == ""' (Go allows == comparison between a named string type and an untyped string literal). The conversion is harmless but slightly inconsistent with how other parts of the codebase use Family values. In the same file, SchemeHuggingFace and SchemePURL branches use string(r.ID) which is correct since ModelID is also a named string type and fmt.Sprintf accepts it either way. The Family comparison in formatCanonical should use r.Family == "" for consistency and to avoid future confusion about whether the type conversion is meaningful.
title: MINOR: formatCanonical() uses string(r.Family) == empty check instead of r.Family == empty Family

close_reason: UAT-2 REVISE items (C1 Version field, C3 3 Resolve bugs, C5 flag/casing/double-underscore, C6 provenance IsValid) delivered across Waves 1-5, verified in code; ACCEPTED at UAT-6 (bestiary-epck).
description:
---
references:
  request: bestiary-n6x
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  impl_plan: bestiary-0ip
---

## Components Demonstrated

### Component 1: Parse + ModelInfo normalized fields
Demonstrated: bestiary show claude-opus-4-5-20251101 returned Family=claude-opus → Normalized=(claude, opus), Date=2025-11-01.
User raised: "What is Family and Variant supposed to mean fundamentally? Opus 4.5 and Opus 4.6 are fundamentally different models."
Decision: REVISE — add Version field to ModelRef (7 fields: ID, Provider, RawFamily, Family, Variant, Version, Date). Parser must extract version (4.5, 4.6) separately from date.

### Component 2: Format dispatch (CanonicalScheme)
Demonstrated: 4 schemes work; SchemeCanonical loses version info (depends on C1 fix).
User clarification: SchemeCanonical with Version should be `provider/family/variant/4.5@date` (dot separator, not hyphen).
Decision: ACCEPT (with Version update tied to Component 1).

### Component 3: Resolve
Demonstrated 5 cases; 3 bugs found:
- Resolve('claude/opus@2025-11-01') returns ErrNotFound, should resolve.
- Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5') returns 7 refs but ZERO from Anthropic — PURL namespace ignored.
- Resolve('claude') returns ErrNotFound, should be ErrAmbiguous with candidates.
Plus: same raw ID across providers shows inconsistent decompositions (qihang-ai/claude/opus vs nano-gpt/claude with no variant) — suggests parsing or codegen data bug.
Decision: REVISE — fix all 3 bugs.

### Component 4: AcceptabilityRating + Designation
Demonstrated: 4 designations per ModelRef, all AcceptabilityAdmitted, typed Scheme enum.
User: "Will need to include version, but this is okay."
Decision: ACCEPT (with Version inclusion tied to Component 1).

### Component 5: SQLite + Model_ constants + bestiary-gen flags
Demonstrated: 8654 Model_ constants for 4325 models; single-hyphen flags work; double-hyphen silently ignored.
User feedback: 3 changes:
1. Fix double-hyphen flag parsing.
2. 'chatgpt' should become 'ChatGPT' typed constant (casing override).
3. Use DOUBLE underscores between fields: Model__<provider>__<family>__<variant>__<version>__<date>.
Decision: REVISE.

### Component 6: Migration docs + provenance integration
Demonstrated: MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md (272 lines); migrations/v2_to_v3.sql exists; provenance Provider.IsValid does only non-empty check with comment 'leaf-package layering; no parent-package import'.
User: "Reject — must delegate to bestiary.IsKnown()". URD R9 explicitly required this.
Decision: REVISE — provenance validation must delegate to bestiary; lift to a higher layer where the import is allowed.

## Final Decision
REVISE — 4 components need fixes (1, 3, 5, 6); 2 and 4 are ACCEPT-with-note dependent on Component 1.
title: UAT-2: Implementation acceptance for entity normalization

IMPORTANT finding. Slice: bestiary-99m. Reviewer C round 1. Severity group: bestiary-mpyc.

The impl plan (bestiary-0ip) specifies 'Models() []ModelID returns all generated constants (defensive copy)' and integration point IP-6 documents 'Models() API'. The worker implemented ModelIDs() instead. constants_test.go also calls ModelIDs(), not Models(). This is a spec deviation: the public API name matters because downstream callers (SLICE-6, provenance companion) will reference it by name. The function should be named Models() to match the spec.

Additionally, this function is SLICE-4 scope (bestiary-ktj). Even if the stub form is acceptable, the function name must be resolved before SLICE-4 lands its correct implementation.

How to fix: If the stub survives the BLOCKER fix (Option B), rename ModelIDs() to Models() everywhere — in models_constants_gen.go and constants_test.go — before SLICE-4 takes ownership. SLICE-4 should then emit Models() in its generated file.

## Scope (Layer 2: Tests — provenance repo)

### pkg/ptypes/enums_test.go (or similar)
- Given a known provider (e.g. "anthropic") when `.IsValid()` then true
- Given an unknown provider ("foo") when `.IsValid()` then false
- Given empty string when `.IsValid()` then false
- Given case-mismatched ("ANTHROPIC") when `.IsValid()` then false (bestiary is case-sensitive)

description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-99m
  reviewer: reviewer-A
  round: 1
  leaf_of: bestiary-lz4e
---
## Finding

PROPOSAL-3 deliverable 8 specifies the public API addition as:

> `Models() []ModelID` — returns all generated constants as a defensive copy.

The worker implemented `ModelIDs() []ModelID` instead.

## Worker Rationale (documented in codegen template)

`registry.go` already declares `Models() []ModelInfo`. Go does not allow two functions with the same name in the same package (even with different return types — no overloading). Adding `Models() []ModelID` to `models_constants_gen.go` would be a compile error. The worker renamed it to `ModelIDs()` to avoid the conflict.

## Why IMPORTANT (not BLOCKER)

The rename is technically correct and necessary to compile. The PROPOSAL-3 spec failed to account for the pre-existing `Models() []ModelInfo` in `registry.go`. The worker's decision is sound. However the spec name deviation means:
1. `constants_test.go` (untracked, SLICE-4-owned) consistently uses `ModelIDs()` throughout — the test and implementation are consistent.
2. The public API surface now has an asymmetry: `Models()` returns metadata, `ModelIDs()` returns IDs only.

## Recommendation

Accept the rename. Update PROPOSAL-3 deliverable 8 and any downstream documentation to use `ModelIDs()` instead of `Models()`. The supervisor should note this in the epoch's architectural log so SLICE-4 and SLICE-6 workers do not revert to `Models()`.

The existing `registry.go:Models()` function should not be renamed (it is existing public API, a breaking change).
title: IMPORTANT: ModelIDs() deviates from PROPOSAL-3 spec which says Models() []ModelID

Wave 2: SLICE-2 (Wire+Client) + SLICE-3 (SQLite Store) — parallel, after Wave 1
Wave 3: SLICE-4 (Codegen+Registry) — after SLICE-2 merged (needs client for codegen)
Wave 4: SLICE-5 (Merge+Format+CLI) — after all slices merged (wires everything)

### Vertical Slices

| Slice | Task ID | Files | Depends On |
|-------|---------|-------|------------|
| SLICE-1: Core Types + Module Init | bestiary-nry | go.mod, bestiary.go, provider.go, modality.go, errors.go | (none) |

### Leaf Tasks per Slice

IP-2: Client API (Owner: SLICE-2, Consumers: SLICE-4 codegen, SLICE-5 CLI sync)

## Components Reviewed

### Component 1: Provider Constants
**Definition shown:** providers_gen.go with ~110 Provider constants, slugToIdentifier with casing override table
**Example shown:** ProviderMistral, ProviderXAI, Provider302AI, ProviderSAPAICore
**Question:** Is the naming strategy (casing override table) right?
**Options:** Override table approach is correct (Known abbreviations get special casing, everything else PascalCase), Use API 'name' field for casing hints (derive from human-readable name), Always strict PascalCase no overrides (simpler, less readable)
**User response:** "Something between (1) and (2) look correct" — blend API name field with override table as fallback
**Decision:** ACCEPT

### Component 2: ModelRef Type
**Definition shown:** 3-field ModelRef (Provider, RawFamily, Date), Ref() method, ProvidersForFamily()
**Question:** Is 3-field scaffolding the right foundation, or include empty Family/Variant now?
**Options:** 3-field MVP is right, Include empty Family/Variant now (prevents breaking type change), Skip ModelRef entirely
**User response:** Include empty Family/Variant now
**Decision:** REVISE — expand to 5 fields (Provider, RawFamily, Family, Variant, Date)

### Component 3: Configurable Filter
**Definition shown:** -providers and -exclude-providers flags, mutual exclusivity, filter asymmetry (constants always generated)
**Question:** Is filter asymmetry correct?
**Options:** Filter asymmetry is correct, Filter should apply to both, No filter needed for MVP
**User response:** Filter asymmetry is correct
**Feedback:** "-providers should be named -only-providers"
**Additional feedback (later):** "-exclude-providers should be -all-providers-except"
**Decision:** ACCEPT with flag renames

### Component 4: Model Constants Deferral
**Definition shown:** No model ID constants for MVP, string access pattern unchanged
**Question:** Is deferring model constants to normalization epoch acceptable?
**Options:** Defer is correct, Generate per-provider model constants now, Generate family-level constants now
**User response:** Generate family-level constants now
**Decision:** ACCEPT — add family-level constants (FamilyClaudeOpus, FamilyGPT4o, etc.) to scope

### Component 5: Test Strategy
**Definition shown:** Property-based tests + regression guards replacing count-based assertions
**Question:** Is property + guard balance right?
**Options:** Property + guard balance is right, Add golden file test too
**User response:** Both — property + guard AND golden file test
**Decision:** ACCEPT with golden file addition

### Catch-all: Missing Requirements
**Question:** Anything missing from original vision?
**User response:** Two missing requirements:
1. Collision documentation — the 62 model ID collisions should be documented thoroughly (full enumeration, not just pattern examples)
2. API response caching — proposal doesn't address caching the models.dev JSON response for offline analysis/exploration

## Summary of Revisions Required
1. ModelRef: 5 fields (Provider, RawFamily, Family, Variant, Date) — Family/Variant empty until normalization
2. Flag renames: -only-providers and -all-providers-except
3. Family-level constants: Generate FamilyClaudeOpus, FamilyGPT4o from API data
4. Golden file test: Snapshot provider list alongside property tests
5. Naming strategy: Blend API name field with casing override table
6. Collision documentation: Full enumeration of all 62 model ID collisions
7. API response caching: Cache models.dev JSON for offline analysis

## Final Decision
ACCEPT with noted revisions (7 items above)
title: UAT-1: Plan acceptance for codegen provider constants

description:
## What

`doubao-seed-1-6-thinking-250715` has `Modifier: ""` in `models_static_gen.go` but its constant is `Model__302AI__Doubao__Seed__1__6__Thinking__250715`. The `__Thinking__` in the constant name comes from raw token splitting (thinking is a mid-ID token, not trailing), NOT from the `Modifier` field. This is an inconsistency: the constant name implies a thinking modifier but `ModelInfo.Modifier` is empty.

## Why IMPORTANT (not BLOCKER)

The slice spec documented "250715 handling = worker's call". The worker explicitly handled this by having ExtractModifier only match when modifier IS the trailing token (250715 is trailing, not thinking). The behavior is correct per the spec scope decision. The inconsistency between constant naming (token-splitting always includes thinking) and the field (empty) is a readability/correctness concern for future callers who iterate constants and expect `Modifier` to be non-empty when `__Thinking__` appears in the constant name.

## Impact

A caller who:
```go
// Find all "thinking" models
for _, m := range bestiary.StaticModels() {
    if m.Modifier == "thinking" { ... }
}
```
will MISS `doubao-seed-1-6-thinking-250715` even though the constant contains `__Thinking__`.

## Recommendation (FOLLOWUP)

Either:
1. Add a pre-strip pass in ExtractModifier: if the trailing token is a YYMMDD date, strip it first, THEN check for modifier (so `-thinking-250715` → strip -250715 → check -thinking → Modifier="thinking").
2. OR document the YYMMDD ambiguity explicitly in the `parse/data/modifiers.json` comment.

Route to bestiary-602y FOLLOWUP epic.
title: IMPORTANT: doubao-seed-1-6-thinking-250715 has Modifier="" in ModelInfo but __Thinking__ in constant name

## Scope
Author resolve.go:
- type ResolveOption func(*resolveConfig)
- WithScheme(s CanonicalScheme) ResolveOption — pin scheme; default = auto-detect by prefix.
- Resolve(input string, opts ...ResolveOption) ([]ModelRef, error) — implements URD R6 / Reviewer C-N1 contract.

Resolve algorithm:
1. Detect or apply explicit Scheme.
2. Match against StaticModels() (and SQLite cache if available).
3. Group matches by Canonical = (NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant, NormalizedDate).
4. If 0 matches: return ErrNotFound{What:"model", Key:input}.
5. If matches share 1 distinct Canonical: return []ModelRef from each match's Ref(), err=nil (cross-provider hosting).
6. If matches span >= 2 distinct Canonicals: return *ErrAmbiguous with candidate list, err = &ErrAmbiguous{...}.

Author resolve_test.go covering all four code paths plus auto-detect by prefix.

## Files Owned
- resolve.go
- resolve_test.go
title: SLICE-3-L5: resolve.go — Resolve + ResolveOption

## Scope

Implement all behavior to make L2 tests pass. End-to-end vertical: parser → ModelInfo → ModelRef → format → store → codegen → regen.

## Files Owned

- `parse.go` — Add Version extraction. Choice (worker decides):
  (a) New public function `ExtractVersion(raw Family) string` that runs after ParseFamily, returns version string ('4.5', '2.5', '4o') extracted from the variant or family tail; OR
  (b) Refactor ParseFamily to return `(Family, variant, version)` triple — additive overload `ParseFamilyV2` to preserve existing callers, then migrate internally.
  Update `parse/data/version_patterns.json` to use clearer named regex groups for version separation.
- `modelref.go` — Update formatCanonical to include Version. Cases:
  - Family=\"\" → \"<provider>/<id>\" (unchanged fallback)
  - Variant=\"\", Version=\"\", Date=\"\" → \"<provider>/<family>\"
  - Variant=\"\", Version=\"\", Date set → \"<provider>/<family>@<date>\"
  - Variant set, Version=\"\", Date=\"\" → \"<provider>/<family>/<variant>\"
  - Variant set, Version=\"\", Date set → \"<provider>/<family>/<variant>@<date>\"
  - Variant set, Version set, Date=\"\" → \"<provider>/<family>/<variant>/<version>\"
  - Variant set, Version set, Date set → \"<provider>/<family>/<variant>/<version>@<date>\"
  - Variant=\"\", Version set, Date=\"\" → \"<provider>/<family>/<version>\"  (uncommon but possible)
  - Variant=\"\", Version set, Date set → \"<provider>/<family>/<version>@<date>\"
  Update ModelInfo.Ref() to map NormalizedVersion → ModelRef.Version.
- `bestiary.go` — wire NormalizedVersion field (already added in L1). Doc comment.
- `store.go` — implement migrateToV4 in the migration switch: ALTER TABLE models ADD COLUMN version TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''; DROP INDEX idx_canonical; CREATE INDEX idx_canonical ON models(family, variant, version, provider); update upsert + scan; bump CanonicalFilter to include Version.
- `migrations/v3_to_v4.sql` — match the in-Go migration; standalone SQL reference.
- `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` (`genToModelInfo`, lines 616-674) — call ExtractVersion (or use new ParseFamilyV2) and populate ModelInfo.NormalizedVersion. Apply both for empty-raw-family fallback (InferFamilyFromID path) AND for non-empty case.
- `bestiary.schema.json` — already updated in L1; verify the conformance test passes.
- Run `go generate ./...` to regenerate models_static_gen.go (now with NormalizedVersion populated) AND models_constants_gen.go (constant naming stays single-underscore until SLICE-FIX-3 — VERSION SEGMENT IS NOT YET ADDED TO CONSTANT NAMES IN THIS SLICE).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Given L2 tests, when CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./... runs, then all pass.
- Given an existing v3 database, when OpenStore runs, then v3→v4 migration completes, version column added with default '', idx_canonical rebuilt should never lose data or fail.
- Given the regenerated models_static_gen.go, when grep'd for known versioned models, then NormalizedVersion is populated (e.g., for claude-opus-4-5 entries, NormalizedVersion=\"4.5\").
- Given go vet ./... and CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./..., when run, then all pass.
- Given git diff, when reviewed, then no model constant in models_constants_gen.go has changed format yet (still single underscore — SLICE-FIX-3's job).

## Commit

- Commit using `git agent-commit -m \"...\"` (NEVER `git commit`).
- Commit small/often: type changes, parser, format, store migration, codegen wiring, regen.
- Stay on branch `main` (do NOT push).

description:
references:
  review: bestiary-fw9w
  slice: bestiary-0qak

## Finding 1: No CHANGELOG.md exists

The commit message and worker notes both flag that removing pkg/ptypes.Provider.IsValid() is a breaking change that 'Needs CHANGELOG entry for the next provenance tag (supervisor action).' However, there is no CHANGELOG.md (or RELEASE-NOTES.md) at the provenance repo root at all. The worker delegated this to the supervisor.

For an end-user consuming the provenance package as a library, a breaking change with no CHANGELOG leaves them with no place to look for migration guidance. The file should at minimum be scaffolded with a ## Unreleased section noting the breaking change.

Suggestion: supervisor or follow-up task should create CHANGELOG.md with an Unreleased section documenting: 'Breaking: pkg/ptypes.Provider.IsValid() removed. Migrate callers to provenance.IsValid(p ptypes.Provider) bool in the root package.'

## Finding 2: IsKnown doc comment does not document empty/whitespace behavior

provenance.IsKnown doc says only 'reports whether p is a member of the bestiary provider catalog.' It does not mention that empty and whitespace-only strings return false. Since IsValid's doc explicitly covers this case, and IsKnown sits right next to it, callers need to understand whether they can pass arbitrary strings to IsKnown without pre-checking. The implicit behavior (false for empty) should be noted.

Suggestion: Add to IsKnown doc: 'Returns false for empty strings and any value not in the bestiary catalog.' This parallels the explicitness of IsValid.
title: SLICE-FIX-4-REVIEW-C-1 MINOR: No CHANGELOG.md scaffolded; IsKnown empty-string behavior undocumented

## Components Reviewed

### Component 1: ModelRef + Format dispatch (6-field struct, CanonicalScheme enum)
User response: "Shape is correct" + clarifying questions about HuggingFace/PURL formats.
Decision: ACCEPT shape; revise format definitions per Option A direction (canonical CAN be a provider's representation; don't invent synthetic format).
Variant decomposition must handle hyphen-separated versions (claude-opus-4-5-20251101 → Family=claude, Variant=opus-4-5, Date=2025-11-01).

### Component 2: Resolve and ErrAmbiguous
Decision: ACCEPT — split between "cross-provider hosting → []ModelRef" vs "multiple distinct canonicals → ErrAmbiguous" is correct.

### Component 3: AcceptabilityRating + Designation
Decision: ACCEPT — three-value enum (Admitted/Preferred/Deprecated), all start as Admitted, promotion deferred to curation epoch.

### Component 4: Decomposition data files (decompose/data/)
User response: "Shouldn't decomposition be called parsing?"
Decision: REVISE — rename everything: decompose.go → parse.go, DecomposeFamily → ParseFamily, decompose/data/ → parse/data/. Full rename across proposal.

### Component 5: SQLite v2→v3 migration
User response: "Why do we have this normalized_* prefix? the columns should just store the normalized data by default."
Decision: REVISE — column renaming. Existing `family` → `raw_family`. New columns: `family` (parsed), `variant`, `date`. Index `(family, variant, provider)`.

### Component 6: Model ID constants
User responses:
- "claude-opus-4-1 is not Claude Opus V41. It is Claude Opus 4.1."
- "These should be Model_Anthropic_Claude_Opus_4_20250514. Similar with the Sonnet."
- "Model_OpenAI_GPT_4o_20240806"
- "Let's also overhaul the rest of the constant enums to be like this."
- Then clarification: "Only Model constants get underscores. We don't need to replace hyphens or dots with underscores."
Decision: REVISE — only Model constants use underscore-separated style. Underscores are component separators from parser output (provider, family, variant, version, date). Provider/Family/Harness stay PascalCase (no breaking change). Examples: Model_Anthropic_Claude_Opus_4_20250514, Model_OpenAI_GPT_4o_20240806.

### Migration files
User: "We should have a migration file too" + "Also have an SQL migration file"
Decision: Create MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md (single doc covering all changes) + separate SQL migration file for the v2→v3 schema migration.

### Catch-all
"All concerns covered"

## Final Decision
ACCEPT with 5 revisions:
1. Format set per Option A direction (canonical can be a provider's actual representation)
2. Hyphen-separated version decomposition (claude-opus-4-5)
3. parse rename (decompose → parse everywhere)
4. SQL column rename (raw_family + family/variant/date, no normalized_* prefix)
5. Model_ underscore naming convention + MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md + SQL migration file
title: UAT-1: Plan acceptance for entity normalization

Deferred from Epoch 1 (codegen determinism) per Plan UAT — depends on Epoch 2 (parser correctness) landing first.

## Scope
1. **Meaningful collision naming** (the original URD R2 'meaningful _N'): replace stable-but-bare _1/_2 with meaningful raw-ID-derived disambiguators. Architect's analysis (PROPOSAL-2/3, bestiary-f0wt/xop7) is reusable: scheme (a) prefix-fragment / (c) hybrid. Re-derive against POST-Epoch-2 collision surface (will differ once version extraction is fixed).
2. **Same-model preferred-appellation collapse** (user insight, verbatim): when two raw IDs are the SAME model differing only by version punctuation (e.g. claude-3.5-haiku vs claude-3-5-haiku on the same provider), they should map to ONE constant, preferring the dot form ('3.5' → __3_5__), NOT two _1/_2 constants. This is an ISO-1087 'preferred designation' decision and requires the parser (Epoch 2) to recognize 3.5 ≡ 3-5 as the same version.

## Depends on
Epoch 2 (parser correctness, bestiary-ydrv) — version extraction must recognize N-M ≡ N.M before the preferred-appellation collapse is implementable.

## Scope (Layer 2: Tests written before impl, will fail until L3)

Add tests covering all 4 Resolve fixes. Use existing resolve_test.go and format_test.go conventions; add new test files where helpful.

### Fix #1: PURL loose-fallback
- Given `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/<id-only-on-other-providers>')` then ErrAmbiguous returned with all-provider candidates AND error message names the missed namespace
- Given `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5')` (matching Anthropic) then returns Anthropic ModelRef cleanly (existing behavior preserved)

### Fix #2: ErrAmbiguous group + truncate
- Given input matching 17+ same-canonical rehosts then ErrAmbiguous candidate list has ONE row per (Family, Variant, Version)
- Given >10 distinct canonical groups then output truncates after 10 with "+M more" hint
- Given exactly 10 groups then no truncation hint emitted

### Fix #3: --format flag
- Given `bestiary show <canonical-input>` (default) then resolves as canonical/peasant
- Given `bestiary show <purl-input>` (default, no --format) then ERROR (no auto-detect)

- Given `bestiary show <hf-input> --format huggingface` (and `--format hf`) then resolves via HF
- Given `bestiary show <raw-id> --format raw` then resolves via raw ID exact match

### Fix #4: Family.CanonicalProvider preference
- Given `Family.CanonicalProvider()` for "claude" then returns Anthropic; for "gemini" Google; for "gpt" OpenAI; for "llama" Local

- Given `Resolve('claude/opus@2025-11-01')` matching Anthropic + 17 rehosts then returns single Anthropic ModelRef
- Given canonical-form input where CanonicalProvider returns empty and >1 provider matches then ErrAmbiguous returned (no preference applied)

## Questions and Responses (URE survey, 2026-05-30)

### Sort key
Q: What sort key should give the static model set (and the collision-suffix assignment) its stable order?
Options: (Provider, ID) [group by provider then raw ID; matches registry composite key; legible diffs]; (ID, Provider) [group identical raw IDs across providers]; (Family, Variant, Version, Date, Provider) [canonical-semantic; couples ordering to decomposition which Epoch 2 changes]
A: **(Provider, ID)**

### Collision suffix
Q: When N models collapse to the same Model__ constant name, how should the _1/_2/_N disambiguation suffix be assigned (deterministically)?
Options: By raw ID alphabetical [simple, stable]; Keep _N but make it meaningful [replace bare _1/_2 with a disambiguating token; scope expansion + churns constant names]; Follow the chosen sort key [assign _N in Q1 sort order]
A: **Keep _N but make it meaningful**  (NOTE: scope addition — collided constant names change to carry a meaningful disambiguator; architect designs the scheme)

### Reproducibility guarantee (multiSelect)
Q: What reproducibility guarantee should this epoch ship?
Options: Unit test (two regens byte-identical); Up-to-date guard (fail if committed gen files differ from fresh --no-fetch regen); Document in AGENTS.md
A: **ALL THREE** — Unit test + Up-to-date guard + Document in AGENTS.md

### Catch-all (multiSelect)
Q: Anything else for the determinism epoch's requirements?
Options: Nothing else — scope is right; Must not change the model set; Keep it minimal
A: **Nothing else — scope is right**
title: ELICIT: Deterministic & reproducible codegen

description:
---
references:
  request: bestiary-n5ul
  elicit: bestiary-r8jw
  seed_bug: bestiary-9lnq
---
## Requirements

### R1: Deterministic model ordering
Sort the `models []ModelInfo` slice by **(Provider, ID)** exactly once, immediately after assembly in `fetchModelsWithRaw` (cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go), so every downstream consumer sees a stable order: `generateSource` (static struct emission), `generateConstantsSource`→`resolveCollisions` (collision-suffix assignment), `collectFamilies`, `countUniqueProviders`. Tiebreak: Provider asc, then ID asc.

### R2: Meaningful collision disambiguation (scope addition, user-selected)
When ≥2 models in a provider collapse to the same `Model__` constant name, replace the bare `_1`/`_2`/`_N` suffix with a **meaningful, deterministic disambiguator** derived from the distinguishing part of the colliding raw IDs (e.g. a normalized fragment of the raw ID path). The architect designs the exact scheme. Requirements on the scheme:
- Deterministic + stable across regens (no run-to-run flip).
- Uniquely distinguishes each colliding member.
- Derived from the raw ID (not positional).
- Valid Go identifier; consistent with existing casing rules.
NOTE: this churns the names of currently-numbered collision constants — an accepted, one-time breaking change for those specific constants.

### R3: Reproducibility unit test
A test that runs the generation pipeline twice against the same fixed input and asserts the two outputs are BYTE-IDENTICAL for both models_static_gen.go and models_constants_gen.go.

### R4: Up-to-date guard test
A test that fails if the committed generated files differ from a fresh `--no-fetch` regen against the cached api_response.json — catches "forgot to regen" drift (CI-style guard).

### R5: Documentation
Document the determinism invariant + how to verify it in the codegen section of AGENTS.md (= CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md symlink).

### R6: No model-set / field changes
Zero change to which models or field values exist. Counts must be unchanged: 4329 models, 115 providers, 169 families, ~527 Version-populated, ~208 Modifier-populated. Only ordering + collision-naming change.

### R7: One-time canonical regen
After the sort + disambiguation land, run `go run ./cmd/bestiary-gen --no-fetch` once and commit the regenerated files in a separate `chore(gen):` commit. Expect a large but semantically-empty diff for models_static_gen.go (pure re-sort) + collision-name changes for the affected constants.

## Priorities
1. R1 (deterministic sort) — foundation; resolves the static-struct shuffle + stabilizes collision assignment.
2. R3 + R4 (reproducibility test + up-to-date guard) — the objective acceptance gates.
3. R2 (meaningful collision disambiguation) — user-requested scope addition; architect designs scheme.
4. R5 (docs), R7 (regen) — accompany.

## Design Choices
- Sort key = (Provider, ID) (matches registry composite key; legible per-provider diffs).
- Determinism via sort-before-emit (standard Go reproducible-codegen; gofmt already deterministic).
- Collision disambiguator is meaningful (not bare _N) per user; raw-ID-derived + stable.

## MVP Goals
Deterministic (Provider,ID) sort + reproducibility unit test + up-to-date guard + one-time regen, with zero model-set change. Released as v0.2.1.

## End-Vision Goals
Reproducible codegen guaranteed in CI (up-to-date guard prevents drift); meaningful collision constants that are stable references.

## Out of Scope (this epoch)
- Parser decomposition correctness (the 121 parse failures) — that is Epoch 2 / v0.2.2.
- Any new CLI flags, model-set changes, or field-semantics changes.

## Release
v0.2.1, landing BEFORE the provenance coordinated-release session and BEFORE Epoch 2 (parser correctness).
title: URD: Deterministic & reproducible codegen

references:

## What
TestParseFamily_HyphenVersion has exactly one case: 'claude-opus-4-5' where base='claude-opus' IS in overrides. The hyphen-version pattern has a branch for when the base is NOT in overrides (parse.go line 249: 'No override for the base; return base as family'). This branch is never exercised.

## Why It Matters
The no-override branch returns (Family(base), variant) directly. If this logic were broken, no test would detect it. The UAT revision 2 explicitly calls out the hyphen-version rule as important (claude-opus-4-5-20251101 handling).

## Where
parse.go lines 246-250 — else branch of the hyphen-version override lookup.
parse_test.go TestParseFamily_HyphenVersion — only one test case, only covers the override branch.

## How to Fix
Add a test case where the hyphen-version base is NOT in overrides:
  Input: 'llama-3-1' (base='llama-3', no overrides entry) → want ('llama-3', '1') OR
  Input: 'foo-bar-2-1' (base='foo-bar', no overrides entry) → want ('foo-bar', '2-1')
Document the expected behavior clearly in the test.

## Requirements

### R1: Variant decomposition
- DecomposeFamily function: takes raw API family value, splits into (Family, Variant)
- Suffix-stripping table approach: known suffixes (-mini, -pro, -flash, -lite, -turbo, -instruct, -base, -ultra, -free, -thinking, -large, -medium, -small, -spark, -nano)
- Manual override file for edge cases that don't fit the suffix pattern
- Deterministic, auditable, easy to fix

### R2: Entity normalization pipeline
- Takes any model ID + provider, decomposes to canonical triple (Family, Variant, Date)
- Rules engine: deterministic suffix stripping + manual overrides
- Handles 25% of models with empty family (via fallback parsing of model ID)
- Date extraction from model ID where present (~3% of models have dates in IDs)
- LRM is inspiration only — no Work/Expression/Manifestation/Nomen types

### R3: Canonical ModelRef and string formats
- ModelRef stays as a struct with (Family, Variant, Date, Provider) — Provider is metadata
- String() method: slash-separated default form (e.g., `anthropic/claude/opus@2025-05-14`)
- StringHuggingFace() method: HuggingFace-style format
- StringPURL() method: PURL ECMA-427:2025 format (e.g., `pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus@2025-05-14`)
- Tuple is canonical; string forms are convenience formatters

### R4: Model ID constants generation
- Codegen produces per-canonical-form constants (e.g., ModelClaudeOpus, ModelClaudeOpus20250514)
- Generated from canonical (Family, Variant, Date) tuples after decomposition
- Resolves the 191 collision pairs by using canonical names instead of raw IDs
- Provider-prefixed where cross-provider collisions occur

### R5: AcceptabilityRating (ISO 1087)
- Enum: AcceptabilityPreferred, AcceptabilityAdmitted, AcceptabilityDeprecated
- ALL designations default to AcceptabilityAdmitted in this epoch
- No "preferred" assignments yet — promotion deferred to follow-up review phase
- No "deprecated" assignments yet
- Each Nomen-equivalent (alias / designation) carries a Rating field

### R6: Runtime CLI normalizer
- Extend `bestiary show` to accept any model expression and normalize
- No separate `normalize` subcommand — unified UX through show
- Output format options shared with existing CLI (json, yaml, table)
- Reuses the same decomposition rules as build-time codegen

### R7: API response caching
- Expose `--cache-dir` flag on `bestiary-gen` (currently writes to .bestiary-gen-cache/ unconditionally)
- Add `--no-fetch` flag for offline mode (use cached response if available)
- Cache used by explorer agents for offline analysis
- Cache invalidation: timestamp-based or manual

### R8: SQLite persistence of normalized fields
- Schema migration v2 → v3 adds: normalized_family, normalized_variant, normalized_date columns
- New index for cross-provider lookup: (normalized_family, normalized_variant, provider)
- Migration preserves existing data; populates new columns from raw values

### R9: Provenance companion PR (coordinated release)
- Provenance approach: keep provenance.Provider as wrapper, delegate IsValid() to bestiary.IsKnown()
- Adapter: provenance.RegistryFromBestiary stays as the conversion boundary
- Provenance imports bestiary, gains the 110-provider known set
- provenance.Provider.UnmarshalText becomes permissive (mirroring bestiary's permissive-at-unmarshal model)
- Coordinated landing: bestiary v0.1.2 release + provenance PR merged together
- Provenance tests updated to remove 4-value assumption

### R10: Schema version
- BestiarySchemaVersion bumped 0.0.1 → 0.1.0
- Do NOT bump to 1.0.0 in this epoch (only on explicit user request)
- bestiary.schema.json updated to document new ModelRef/Canonical structure

### R11: Tests
- DecomposeFamily table-driven tests covering all variant suffixes
- Manual override table tests
- ModelRef.String/StringHuggingFace/StringPURL round-trip tests
- AcceptabilityRating enum tests + default-to-admitted invariant
- Cross-provider lookup tests via new SQLite index
- Property-based tests: every static model decomposes without error
- Existing TestModelInfo_Ref tests updated (Family/Variant become populated, no longer empty)

## Priorities
1. R1+R2 (decomposition + pipeline) — foundation
2. R3 (canonical formats) — public API
3. R4 (model constants) — depends on R1+R2
4. R6 (CLI normalizer) — thin wrapper over R2
5. R5 (AcceptabilityRating) — admitted-only for now
6. R7 (cache flags) — small standalone
7. R8 (SQLite persistence) — schema migration
8. R9 (provenance PR) — coordinated release
9. R10 (schema version) — small
10. R11 (tests) — accompany all above

## Design Choices
- LRM is inspiration only, NOT direct adoption. Goal is canonical representation of model+provider names.
- Simple canonical triple (Family, Variant, Date) — Provider is metadata
- Suffix-stripping + manual overrides for decomposition (deterministic, auditable)
- Tuple as canonical; multiple string formatters for interop (slash, HuggingFace, PURL)
- AcceptabilityRating exists structurally but everything is "admitted" until follow-up review
- SQLite gets normalized columns + cross-provider index
- Provenance keeps its Provider type, gains bestiary's known set via delegation

## MVP Goals
- All 6 deferred items from prior epoch in scope (full plate)
- ModelRef populates Family + Variant via decomposition
- bestiary show normalizes any input
- bestiary-gen has --cache-dir + --no-fetch
- SQLite v3 migration with normalized indices
- Provenance companion PR ready for coordinated release
- Schema version 0.1.0

## End-Vision Goals (Future Epochs)
- Curation phase: promote selected designations from "admitted" to "preferred" (ISO 1087 rating)
- "Deprecated" rating once we have history of deprecation
- Full PURL spec integration with SBOM tooling (CycloneDX, SPDX, OSV vulnerability databases)
- Cross-registry interop: HuggingFace Hub bidirectional mapping

## Deferred to Follow-up Epoch
- Promotion of designations to "preferred" rating
- Curation review phase
- Full SBOM/PURL ecosystem integration

description:
## Finding

The L2 spec (bestiary-0rr5) explicitly requires ParseFamilyWithVersion to cover:
- 'gpt-4o' → (gpt, "", 4o)
- 'chatgpt-4o-latest' → (chatgpt, "", 4o)

Neither case appears in parse_test.go TestParseFamilyWithVersion_Core or any other ParseFamilyWithVersion test function. The only gpt-4o references in parse_test.go are in TestExtractDate_FromID and TestInferFamilyFromID, which test different functions.

These cases are significant because the 'o-suffix' version pattern (4o) is structurally different from the hyphen-numeric patterns (4-5 → 4.5). The BackwardCompat test covers gpt-mini which has no version; it does not exercise gpt-4o. Without these tests, it is unknown whether ParseFamilyWithVersion correctly handles the alphanumeric version suffix.

## Where
- parse_test.go: TestParseFamilyWithVersion_Core (lines 580-620), no gpt-4o case
- bestiary-0rr5 L2 spec, Files Owned section

## How to fix
Add to TestParseFamilyWithVersion_Core or a new test:
  {"gpt-4o", "gpt-4o", "gpt", "", "4o"},
  {"chatgpt-4o-latest", "chatgpt-4o-latest", "chatgpt", "", "4o"},
And verify ParseFamilyWithVersion returns the expected triple.

description:
---
references:
  proposal: bestiary-j5r
  urd: bestiary-0pi
  request: bestiary-64s
---

## VOTE: ACCEPT

### Axis: Correctness
### Round: 1

## Summary

The proposal correctly implements all 8 URD requirements (R1-R8). Types are sound, interfaces are complete, and the overall design is logically consistent. No BLOCKER-level issues found.

## Requirement Traceability

| URD Req | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| R1: Provider (string + constants) | PASS | 4 constants, IsKnown(), MarshalText/UnmarshalText, any slug valid |
| R2: ModelInfo (all 17 API fields) | PASS | 21 struct fields correctly decompose the 17 API fields (cost split into 5, limit into 2, Provider from parent) |
| R3: Typed Modality enum | PASS | Modality int, 5 constants, Modalities struct, String/Marshal/Unmarshal |
| R4: Code-generated static registry | PASS | cmd/bestiary-gen, go:generate, 3 providers, all 4 query functions |
| R5: HTTP client with functional options | PASS | NewClient(opts...), timeout/retries/base URL, FetchModels, FetchModelsByProvider, context.Context |
| R6: Structured error types | PASS | ErrNotFound, ErrAPIUnavailable with actionable messages and Unwrap() |
| R7: CLI with JSON/YAML output | PASS | list/show/sync, --format json|yaml|table, flag-based |
| R8: No external deps | PASS | Internal YAML serializer, stdlib-only, CGO_ENABLED=0 |

## Type Soundness Assessment

- **Provider**: String type with open extensibility is correct per elicitation. UnmarshalText accepting any string is intentional.
- **Modality**: Int enum with 5 values. UnmarshalText correctly rejects unknown modalities. (Note for workers: String() should bounds-check to avoid panic on out-of-range values.)
- **Cost fields as *float64**: Correct — nil semantically distinct from 0.
- **ContextWindow/MaxOutput as int**: Using 0 as sentinel is acceptable (no model has 0 context window). Matches proposal comment "0 if unknown".
- **Registry defensive copies**: All slice-returning functions copy before returning. LookupModel returns by value. No aliasing bugs.
- **sync.Once initialization**: Thread-safe, correct.

## Interface Completeness

- Registry: StaticModels(), LookupModel(), ModelsByProvider(), ModelsByFamily() — all URD query functions present.
- Client: NewClient(), FetchModels(ctx), FetchModelsByProvider(ctx, p) — all URD client methods present with context support.
- Format: FormatModels(io.Writer, ...), FormatModel(io.Writer, ...) — testable via io.Writer.
- No concrete Client interface defined, which is correct Go practice (consumers define interfaces).

## Non-Blocking Observations (IMPORTANT/MINOR)

### IMPORTANT: 10 MB response body limit omitted
URD R5 specifies "Response limit: 10 MB". The proposal's client.go section does not mention this limit. The existing peasant client has this guard. Workers should implement io.LimitReader in FetchModels to prevent unbounded memory allocation from a malformed or malicious response. This is an implementation detail, not a design flaw.

### MINOR: Validation checklist says errors.Is but types require errors.As
Checklist item 17: "ErrNotFound and ErrAPIUnavailable are distinguishable via errors.Is". Since both are struct error types (not sentinel values), the correct stdlib function is errors.As, not errors.Is. The types themselves are correctly designed — only the checklist text is misleading.

### MINOR: Acceptance criterion "when API is reachable" for list command
The acceptance criterion for `bestiary list --provider anthropic --format table` has precondition "when API is reachable". Since `list` should use the static registry (not the network), this precondition is misleading. The `sync` command is the one that needs network access. Workers should clarify that `list` operates offline.

### MINOR: Modality.String() lacks bounds check
The current design indexes into a fixed-size array without checking if the Modality value is in range. Workers should add a bounds check to avoid a potential panic if an invalid Modality value is constructed.

## End-User Alignment

1. **Who are the end-users?** provenance (Go library consumer) and developers (CLI users).
2. **What would end-users want?** Type-safe model metadata without network dependency (provenance); queryable CLI for model lookup (developers). Both covered.
3. **How would this affect them?** Positively — replaces hardcoded SQL seed data with typed, generated, queryable model registry.
4. **Implementation gaps?** 10 MB limit (noted above, not a design gap).
5. **MVP scope?** All R1-R8 in one milestone is appropriate for this module size.
6. **Validation checklist?** 18 items covering vet, test, build, generate, CLI, registry, client, format. Complete and correct (with the minor errors.Is/As note above).

description:
VOTE: ACCEPT — The proposal is well-organized and proportional to the problem complexity.

## Elegance Axis Evaluation

### 1. version.go — Well-organized? YES

Four exported constants in a single file with clear doc comments. No struct, no interface, no constructor. This is the minimum viable design for provenance tracking. The decision to use separate consts rather than a struct is correct — there is no behavior to attach, no method set needed. Pure data constants do not benefit from being wrapped in a struct.

### 2. JSON Schema approach — Makes sense? YES

Correctly scoped to public output types only (not the upstream wire format). This matches the user's explicit design choice: bestiary output is the contract, wire format is an implementation detail. JSON Schema draft-2020-12 is current and well-supported. Placing bestiary.schema.json in the repo root alongside Go source is conventional.

The schema serves two concrete purposes: (a) documenting the contract for consumers, and (b) enabling automated validation of CLI output. Both are real user needs, not speculative.

### 3. Code fixes — Clean? YES

Verified against the codebase:
- **Dead code removal**: writeYAMLModalityList (format.go:129) is confirmed dead — only one reference (its own definition). Clean removal.
- **Docstring fix**: client.go:74 says 'exponential backoff' but the retry logic is linear. Factual correction, no over-engineering.
- **Deterministic sort**: MergeModels (merge.go:36-39) iterates over a map — confirmed non-deterministic. slices.SortFunc by (Provider, ID) is the simplest correct fix.
- **Error surfacing**: cmd/bestiary/main.go:80 uses `cached, _ = store.QueryModels(...)` — silently drops errors. Correctly flagged as IMPORTANT.
- **ctx documentation**: Documenting the zombiezen/sqlite limitation (no per-op context support) is more honest than faking context threading. Proportional response.

### 4. 3-slice decomposition — Well-structured? YES

- **Slice 1 (Schema Versioning)**: New files + go mod tidy — cohesive provenance/versioning unit. Correctly notes go mod tidy should happen first to avoid go.sum conflicts.
- **Slice 2 (Test Coverage)**: All new test code — cohesive testing unit. Independent of Slice 3.
- **Slice 3 (Code Fixes)**: All IMPORTANT/MINOR fixes to existing code — cohesive maintenance unit.

Each slice has a clear theme and minimal cross-dependencies. The slices are parallelizable after Slice 1's go mod tidy.

### 5. End-user alignment

- **No over-engineering**: No premature abstractions, no plugin systems, no config frameworks. Four consts and a schema file.
- **No under-engineering**: Error surfacing is correctly promoted to IMPORTANT. Deterministic output is addressed. Dead code is removed.
- **Complexity proportional**: ~20 small changes for ~20 small problems. The proposal doesn't inflate scope.
- **All URD requirements covered**: F1-F4 (including F4b UpstreamGitRemote amendment), all 8 IMPORTANT (F5), all ~11 MINOR (F6). No gaps.
- **Validation checklist**: 19 items, each mapping to a specific change. Complete and verifiable.

### Tradeoffs — Well-reasoned? YES

All four documented tradeoffs (public-types-only schema, separate consts over struct, sorted merge output, documented ctx limitation) are proportional decisions with clear rationale.
title: FOLLOWUP_PROPOSAL-1-REVIEW-C-1: Elegance review

close_reason: Fixed in commit 4faf1c3: TestMigration_V2toV3_IndexUsed EXPLAIN now uses WHERE family=? AND variant=? AND date=? to match actual QueryByCanonical predicate shape.
description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-est
  reviewer: B
  round: 1
  file: store_migration_test.go
  test: TestMigration_V2toV3_IndexUsed
---
## Finding

TestMigration_V2toV3_IndexUsed runs EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN on:
  WHERE family = ?1 AND variant = ?2 AND provider = ?3

But QueryByCanonical (store.go) generates:
  WHERE family = ?1 [AND variant = ?2] [AND date = ?3]

idx_canonical is defined on (family, variant, provider). It does NOT include the date column.

The IndexUsed test proves idx_canonical exists and SQLite uses it for a query on (family, variant, provider). But it does NOT prove that QueryByCanonical itself uses the index. To verify QueryByCanonical uses the index, the EXPLAIN must reflect the actual query shape with a date predicate, not a provider predicate.

Note: SQLite will still use the (family, variant) prefix of idx_canonical for QueryByCanonical queries; date becomes a residual filter. But the test should assert on the actual query generated by QueryByCanonical, not a manually written approximation.

## Suggested fix

Change the EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN in TestMigration_V2toV3_IndexUsed to match QueryByCanonical's actual predicate:
  WHERE family = ?1 AND variant = ?2 AND date = ?3
This ensures the test remains aligned with implementation as the query evolves.
title: IMPORTANT: IndexUsed test queries (family,variant,provider) but QueryByCanonical queries (family,variant,date)

close_reason: Fixed in commit 4faf1c3: indexSQL constant created and applied in both fresh-DB path and migrateToV3; TestMigration_FreshDB_IndexCreated added.
description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-est
  reviewer: reviewer-A
  round: 1
---
## Finding

store.go's `schemaSQL` constant creates the v3 `models` table with all four columns (raw_family, family, variant, date) but does NOT include a `CREATE INDEX` statement for `idx_canonical`.

The index is only created inside `migrateToV3()`, which is only called for databases that are upgrading from v1/v2. A fresh installation (first `OpenStore` with no prior DB) follows the `!hasModels` branch in `migrateSchema()`, which executes `schemaSQL` and then calls `setSchemaVersion(3)` — but never calls `migrateToV3()`, so `idx_canonical` is never created.

## Impact

- All new user installations (v3 fresh installs) silently lack the index.
- `QueryByCanonical` doc comment says "The query uses idx_canonical for efficient lookup when family is non-empty" — this is false for fresh DBs.
- SQLite falls back to a full table scan; results remain correct but performance degrades as the models table grows.
- `TestMigration_V2toV3_IndexUsed` only exercises the migration code path; there is no test for the fresh-DB path.

## Fix

Add `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_canonical ON models(family, variant, provider)` immediately after `sqlitex.ExecuteTransient(conn, schemaSQL, nil)` in the `!hasModels` branch of `migrateSchema()`. Alternatively, extract a shared `indexSQL` constant and call it from both the fresh-DB path and `migrateToV3`.
title: IMPORTANT: idx_canonical not created for fresh v3 databases (schemaSQL gap)

---

## Purpose

UAT-2 (bestiary-ozew) returned REVISE on 4 components. The original IMPL_PLAN (bestiary-0ip) is CLOSED; this FIX_IMPL_PLAN drives the corrective wave without reopening prior work.

## Components Requiring Fix

- **Component 1**: ModelRef gains 7th field `Version` (ID, Provider, RawFamily, Family, Variant, Version, Date). Parser must extract version separately from Date. Canonical key for granular identity: (Family, Variant, Version, Date). Cross-snapshot grouping: (Family, Variant, Version). SchemeCanonical format: `provider/family/variant/4.5@date` (dot separator inside version, slash between variant and version, @ before date).
- **Component 3**: 3 Resolve bugs + 1 data-consistency concern.
  1. `Resolve('claude/opus@2025-11-01')` → ErrNotFound; should resolve.
  2. `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5')` → 0 Anthropic hits; PURL provider filter ignored.
  3. `Resolve('claude')` → ErrNotFound; should be ErrAmbiguous with candidates.
  4. Inconsistent decompositions for same raw ID across providers.
- **Component 5**: bestiary-gen flag/casing/naming changes.
  1. Accept `--no-fetch` (double-hyphen) in addition to `-no-fetch`.
  2. Add `chatgpt → ChatGPT` casing override.
  3. Switch Model_ constants to DOUBLE underscores between fields: `Model__<provider>__<family>__<variant>__<version>__<date>`.
- **Component 6**: Provenance must delegate `Provider.IsValid()` to `bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown()`. Lift validation out of `pkg/ptypes` into a higher provenance layer where the bestiary import is allowed.

Components 2 and 4 are ACCEPT-with-note; they update mechanically when Component 1 lands (Version field appears in Format dispatch and Designation output).

## Vertical Slices

- **SLICE-FIX-1** — Version field (Component 1). Foundational. Must land before SLICE-FIX-2 and SLICE-FIX-3 finalize.
- **SLICE-FIX-2** — Resolve bugs (Component 3). Depends on SLICE-FIX-1 contract for Version.
- **SLICE-FIX-3** — Codegen flag + casing + double-underscore (Component 5). Depends on SLICE-FIX-1 (Version field appears in Model_ name template).
- **SLICE-FIX-4** — Provenance delegation (Component 6). Independent of 1/3/5. Coordinated release at the end.

## Layer Integration Points

- **IP-1: ModelRef shape (SLICE-FIX-1 owns; consumed by SLICE-FIX-2, SLICE-FIX-3)** — 7-field struct + canonical key tuple. Shared contract: `modelref.go` exports. Merge timing: SLICE-FIX-1 lands first; downstream slices rebase atop.
- **IP-2: Parse output (SLICE-FIX-1 owns; consumed by SLICE-FIX-2, SLICE-FIX-3)** — `ParseFamily` / version extraction signature. Shared contract: `parse.go` exports.
- **IP-3: Model_ constant naming template (SLICE-FIX-3 owns; consumed by SLICE-FIX-2 indirectly via test fixtures)** — double-underscore separator + new Version segment.
- **IP-4: bestiary public API surface (SLICE-FIX-1 + SLICE-FIX-2)** — what provenance.Provider.IsValid sees. SLICE-FIX-4 consumer; merge timing: provenance fix lands AFTER bestiary slices ship + tag.

## Constraints

- No `git commit` — `git agent-commit -m "..."` only.
- Tests gated by `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...` and `go vet ./...`.
- Do NOT push; commits stay local on `main`.
- Do NOT reopen bestiary-0ip; do NOT close any prior CLOSED task.
- Schema version stays `0.0.2` (this is a fix wave).

## Scope (option b — test+docs ONLY; NO regen, NO generated-output change)
Resolves the LastSynced review finding by making the determinism guarantee HONEST and the test cover the FULL pipeline.

1. R3 TestCodegen_Reproducible_ByteIdentical: currently runFixtureCodegen (~main_test.go L1916) bypasses run()-level LastSynced stamping (LastSynced="" -> trivially identical). FIX: exercise the stamping path — stamp models[i].LastSynced with an INJECTED timestamp (mirror run() main.go:363-365), use TWO DIFFERENT timestamps across the N iterations, and assert bytes.Equal AFTER normalizing (regex-replacing) the LastSynced value. Keep the ordering/collision determinism assertions. Add an assertion that, WITHOUT normalization, two differently-stamped runs differ ONLY in LastSynced lines (proves LastSynced is the sole residual).
2. R4 TestCodegen_UpToDate guard: normalize/strip LastSynced on BOTH sides (committed golden + fresh regen) before comparing, so the guard is a true content compare insensitive to the codegen wall-clock.
3. AGENTS.md determinism subsection: state the guarantee precisely — "regens are byte-identical MODULO the LastSynced codegen timestamp (wall-clock); ordering + collision _N are fully deterministic." Reference FOLLOWUP bestiary-vq6k for true zero-diff (deterministic LastSynced).

## Out of scope: do NOT change generated files, do NOT change LastSynced stamping in run() (that is FOLLOWUP bestiary-vq6k / option a), do NOT touch parser/model-set. ONE commit (test+docs).

## Acceptance
- R3 now drives the run()-equivalent stamping path; passes with LastSynced-normalization; would FAIL if ordering/collision determinism regressed.
- R4 guard insensitive to LastSynced.
- AGENTS.md honestly scopes the guarantee + references bestiary-vq6k.
- go test ./... + go vet ./... pass. No generated-file diff.

close_reason: Already resolved. apiURL was changed from const to package-level var. Both TestCacheDirFlag (main_test.go:562) and TestNoFetch_HitsCache (main_test.go:625) now correctly override apiURL = srv.URL. The HTTP guard is live, not dead code. Verified at HEAD: main_test.go lines 560-563, 623-626.
description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ektc
  reviewer: B (Test Quality)
  round: 1
  file: cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go
  lines: 279-285, 329-333
---
## Finding

Both TestCacheDirFlag and TestNoFetch_HitsCache open an httptest.Server but never wire it to apiURL. Since apiURL is a package-level const, srv.URL cannot override it, and the server is never contacted in either test. This means:

1. TestCacheDirFlag: The server is pure dead code. A reader following the comment 'Serve a minimal API response over HTTP' will spend time understanding why the server is opened but not used.

2. TestNoFetch_HitsCache: The 'contacted' sentinel works correctly (fetchModelsWithRaw with noFetch=true does not call apiURL at all), but the assert 'if contacted' provides no additional safety — the server could be removed and the test would still pass with identical guarantees. The false impression is that the test 'would catch' a regression where --no-fetch still contacts the network, but it would only catch it if apiURL pointed at srv.URL.

## Impact

Misleading test structure creates cognitive load for future maintainers and could hide the lack of true network-isolation testing. If apiURL is ever changed to a var (the correct fix for TestCacheDirFlag BLOCKER), the test would need updating to wire srv.URL anyway.

## How to fix

Either:
- Remove the dead httptest.Server from both tests (simplest, no behavioural loss)
- OR (preferred, resolves BLOCKER too): make apiURL a package-level var, wire srv.URL in both tests, and test the full code path end-to-end via run()
title: IMPORTANT: Dead httptest.Server in TestCacheDirFlag and TestNoFetch_HitsCache creates misleading test intent

---
## Finding

When a user calls `Resolve("claude-opus-4-20250514", WithScheme(SchemeCanonical))`, the function returns `*ErrAmbiguous` with 2 candidates instead of `[]ModelRef` with cross-provider hosting. Root cause: providers have inconsistent NormalizedVariant for the same model ID (e.g. jiekou has variant='opus', nano-gpt has variant='').

### Reproduction
```go
refs, err := Resolve("claude-opus-4-20250514", WithScheme(SchemeCanonical))
// err = *ErrAmbiguous with candidates:
//   jiekou/claude/opus@2025-05-14
//   nano-gpt/claude@2025-05-14
```

## Why It's a BLOCKER

This violates the C-N1 disambiguation contract: an exact raw model ID should always resolve to cross-provider hosting ([]ModelRef, nil err), regardless of which CanonicalScheme is active. The current SchemeCanonical grouping by (family, variant, date) triplet silently treats normalization gaps as distinct canonicals, giving users a misleading ErrAmbiguous on an exact, unambiguous model ID.

## Impact

Any caller who passes an explicit SchemeCanonical with a full model ID (e.g. from a CLI flag --scheme=canonical with a known model ID) gets an error instead of results. This breaks the Slice 6 CLI flow where --scheme=canonical + exact model ID is a natural usage pattern.

## Suggested Fix

In `matchModels`/grouping logic for SchemeCanonical: when the input exactly matches a model ID (string(m.ID) == matchInput), treat it as ID-grouped (same as SchemeRaw) rather than canonical-triple grouped. Only apply canonical-triple grouping when the input is NOT an exact ID (i.e. it is a family-level lookup like "claude").

Concretely, in `Resolve`: after collecting matches, check if any match has `string(ref.ID) == matchInput`. If yes, group by ID (cross-provider). If no exact match, group by (family, variant, date) canonical triple for the ambiguity check.

## Code Location

`/resolve.go` lines 86-115: the grouping logic.
title: BLOCKER: Resolve(SchemeCanonical, exact-ID) returns ErrAmbiguous when NormalizedVariant is inconsistent across providers
