Final commits: 096badd (L1), 4af7e90 (L2), c3764c6+0133f75+7e4bcc1+6ca6a23 (L3 4 fixes), 0400736+130d220 (cycle-2 fix), a90d1d9 (cycle-3 fix). Closes bestiary-e2uk + bestiary-b8o8.
description:
---
references:
  fix_plan_v2: bestiary-2xaf
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  uat: bestiary-g9ci
  followup_canonical_providers: bestiary-1wy7
  resolved_findings:
    - bestiary-e2uk  # extractCanonicalProviderHint stub — V2-2 fix #4 lands the impl
---
## Specification (Component 3 — UAT-3 REVISE)

Four required fixes to Resolve. Verbatim user responses captured in UAT-3 (`bestiary-g9ci`).

### Fix #1: PURL loose-match fallback (verbatim user response)
> "State that we are doing loose matching in the fallback. In zero match case, state that there are none and fallback to ErrAmbiguous with candidates"

When `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/<ns>/<id>')` has zero matches in namespace:
1. Message MUST state "no matches in namespace <ns>" (or equivalent — keep actionable per [C-actionable-errors])
2. Fall back to `ErrAmbiguous` with all-provider candidates (loose match)

Currently the namespace acts as a hard filter (resolve.go:198-200 strips prefix and uses providerHint as filter). Change behavior: when namespace filter yields 0, log/note the namespace miss and re-search without filter.

### Fix #2: ErrAmbiguous output (verbatim user response)
> "Should do both of these:
>   2. Truncate after N (e.g. 10) with '+M more' hint
>   3. Group by NormalizedFamily/Variant; show one canonical per group"

(Note: post-V2-1, this is grouping by Family/Variant/Version — the renamed fields.)

BOTH:
- (a) Truncate candidate list after N=10 with "+M more" hint
- (b) Group by (Family, Variant, Version) tuple — show ONE canonical row per group

This collapses the 17+ vercel-rehost rows currently shown for `claude` input into one canonical row per (Family, Variant, Version).

Implementation: ErrAmbiguous struct gains either a precomputed grouped+truncated view, or its formatter does the work. Formatter location is likely `format.go` (FormatAmbiguous, called from cmd/bestiary/main.go:122). Worker's call where the grouping logic lives (errors.go vs format.go), but tests must cover both grouping AND truncation behavior.

### Fix #3: NEW `--format` input flag (verbatim user response)
> "these `show` commands... Should only take our normalized representation by default. If we want to use HuggingFace format or PURL format or raw format, need to pass `--format {peasant (ours, default),huggingface,(also hf),purl,raw}` if we want to use others"

`bestiary show` defaults to canonical/peasant input form ONLY. Add `--format {peasant,huggingface|hf,purl,raw}` flag, default `peasant`.

`peasant` = bestiary canonical form (`provider/family/variant/version@date` per existing conventions).

The current Resolve auto-detect (resolve.go:191-236 `detectSchemeWithHint` — PURL via `pkg:` prefix, canonical via slashes) is currently always-on. New behavior:
- Resolve auto-detect path is REMOVED in favor of explicit `--format` selection from CLI
- When `--format` is supplied, Resolve dispatches directly to the matching scheme without auto-detect
- Default `peasant` means: parse input as canonical form ONLY; if it doesn't parse as canonical, return error (do NOT silently try other formats)

**Reconciliation note:** `cmd/bestiary/main.go:26-32` currently has both `--format` (json/yaml/table — OUTPUT format) and `--scheme` (resolve scheme override — INPUT format). User's verbatim ask is for `--format` to mean INPUT format. Worker MUST resolve this naming clash:
  - **Recommended:** rename existing OUTPUT `--format` flag to `--output` (or `-o`), and use `--format` for INPUT scheme as the user requested. Document the rename in MIGRATION doc.
  - **Alternative:** keep `--format` for output, add `--input-format` for input. (Less aligned with user's verbatim ask — flag name was specifically `--format`.)
  - Whichever path: surface the rename clearly in MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md.

### Fix #4: NEW canonical-provider preference (verbatim user response)
> "Why this show provider 'qihang-ai' [in claude/opus@2025-11-01 result] — Anthropic should be the canonical provider here"

When canonical-form input matches multiple providers (e.g. Anthropic primary + 17 rehosts), Resolve must prefer the canonical/originating provider.

**Mechanism (locked by team-lead):**
- Add `func (f Family) CanonicalProvider() Provider` method in `family.go` (NOT in families_gen.go — that's auto-generated).
- Body is a static map (switch or map literal).
- Empty Provider on unknown family — Resolve falls back to ErrAmbiguous with all candidates (no panic).

**Wave 3 scope (verbatim user direction, UAT-3 Decision 2 catch-all):**
> "For now, we can just determine the canonical providers for the most popular models and stub the rest with a placeholder value, with a comment specifically stating that the placeholder will be reviewed and filled in during a followup."

Populate the well-known mappings:
- `claude` → `Anthropic`
- `gemini` → `Google`
- `gemma` → `Google`
- `gpt` → `OpenAI`
- `chatgpt` → `OpenAI`
- `o1`, `o3` → `OpenAI`
- `llama` → `Local` (per team-lead direction)
- Plus other clear cases the worker identifies (mistral, qwen, deepseek where confident — worker's call)

Unknowns: single sentinel return path with comment block:
```go
// TODO(followup bestiary-1wy7): review canonical provider for this family — see FOLLOWUP_SLICE-2
default:
    return "" // empty Provider; Resolve falls back to ErrAmbiguous
```

Placeholder MUST NOT be a wrong-but-plausible guess. Empty Provider only.

### Resolve flow change

When canonical-form input matches >1 provider:
1. Look up `Family.CanonicalProvider()`
2. If non-empty AND that provider is among match set → return single ModelRef from that provider
3. If empty OR not among matches → ErrAmbiguous with all candidates (existing behavior, but enhanced with Fix #2 grouping+truncation)

## Files Owned

- `resolve.go`: replace `extractCanonicalProviderHint` stub (lines 285-292) with real impl OR call `Family.CanonicalProvider()` from a new step in the Resolve dispatcher; PURL loose-fallback in providerHint filter logic (lines 87-92, 198-200)
- `family.go`: NEW `CanonicalProvider()` method with static map + TODO placeholder
- `errors.go`: ErrAmbiguous struct may gain grouped/truncated view (worker's call)
- `format.go`: `FormatAmbiguous` updated for grouping + truncation
- `cmd/bestiary/main.go`: `--format` input flag (lines 26-32 area), reconciliation with existing output flag
- `MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md`: document --format flag, output-flag rename if any, canonical-provider behavior
- Tests: `resolve_test.go`, `format_test.go`, new tests for `Family.CanonicalProvider`, CLI integration tests

## Leaf Tasks
- SLICE-FIX-V2-2-L1: Types — Family.CanonicalProvider method skeleton; ErrAmbiguous shape changes if any; CLI flag wiring stub
- SLICE-FIX-V2-2-L2: Tests — TDD for all 4 fixes
- SLICE-FIX-V2-2-L3: Impl — Resolve dispatch changes, formatter grouping+truncation, --format flag handling

## Dependencies
- BLOCKED-BY V2-1: tests reference renamed ModelInfo fields; grouping is by renamed (Family, Variant, Version)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/<unknown-id>')` when no anthropic matches when there ARE matches under other providers then return ErrAmbiguous with message naming the missed namespace AND with all-provider candidates, should never silently return zero results without a namespace-miss diagnostic
- Given `Resolve('claude')` returning ErrAmbiguous when 17+ rehosts exist for same canonical (claude, opus, *) then candidate list shows ONE row per (Family, Variant, Version) tuple, should never return >1 row per canonical group
- Given an ErrAmbiguous candidate list with >10 distinct canonical groups when formatted then output truncates after 10 with "+M more" hint, should never dump a full unbounded list
- Given `bestiary show <input>` with no `--format` flag when input is canonical/peasant form then resolves cleanly, should never auto-detect PURL or HuggingFace
- Given `bestiary show pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5 --format purl` then resolves via PURL scheme, should never silently treat as canonical
- Given `Resolve('claude/opus@2025-11-01')` matching Anthropic + 17 rehosts when Family.CanonicalProvider returns Anthropic then return single Anthropic ModelRef, should never return qihang-ai or other rehost as primary
- Given an unknown family in CanonicalProvider when called then return empty Provider, should never panic; Resolve must fall back to ErrAmbiguous with all candidates
- Given Wave 3 stubs unknown families with TODO comment then `grep -n 'TODO(followup bestiary-1wy7)' family.go` matches; should never leave the placeholder uncommented or misreference the followup task

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] PURL namespace miss → loose-match fallback + diagnostic message
- [ ] ErrAmbiguous formatter groups by (Family, Variant, Version)
- [ ] ErrAmbiguous formatter truncates at 10 + "+M more"
- [ ] `--format {peasant,huggingface|hf,purl,raw}` flag added; default peasant
- [ ] Output flag clash with existing `--format` resolved (rename to `--output` recommended; documented in MIGRATION)
- [ ] `Family.CanonicalProvider()` method added in family.go
- [ ] Popular families populated; rest fall through to empty-Provider with TODO
- [ ] Resolve dispatcher uses CanonicalProvider on canonical-form multi-match
- [ ] `extractCanonicalProviderHint` stub removed/replaced (closes `bestiary-e2uk`)
- [ ] All tests pass; `go vet` clean
- [ ] Atomic commits per layer
title: SLICE-FIX-V2-2: Resolve overhaul (PURL loose-fallback + ErrAmbiguous group/truncate + --format flag + canonical-provider preference)

## Scope (Layer 2: Tests written before impl, fail until L3)

### Failure detection (parse_test.go)
- Given `claude-3-5-haiku-20241022` parsed when version digits between family-prefix and variant cannot be extracted then ParseFailure emitted with reason "version digits between family-prefix and variant not extracted"
- Given a model ID with extra suffix segments beyond family/variant/version/date when parsed then ParseFailure emitted with reason "suffix overflow: extra segments after expected family/variant/version/date"
- Given a Mistral 4-digit numeric (e.g. `mistral-2401`) when YYMM date cannot be cleanly distinguished from version then ParseFailure emitted with reason "YYMM-date-as-version false-positive"
- Given a cleanly parseable model ID (e.g. `claude-opus-4-5-20251101`) when parsed then NO ParseFailure emitted

### File write integration (cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go)
- Given bestiary-gen runs with N failures detected then file `{cacheDir}/parse_failures.json` exists with N records
- Given bestiary-gen runs with zero failures then file exists with valid JSON envelope and `failure_count: 0`, `failures: []`
- Given bestiary-gen runs twice in succession then second run OVERWRITES (not appends) — file contents reflect ONLY the second run
- Given the JSON file when re-parsed via `json.Unmarshal` into ParseFailuresEnvelope then round-trips equal

## Files Owned
- parse_test.go (failure detection cases)
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go (integration cases)

## Acceptance
- Tests COMPILE but FAIL after L2 alone — expected
- Tests use `t.TempDir()` for cache-dir isolation per CLAUDE.md testing conventions
title: SLICE-FIX-V2-3-L2: Tests — failure detection (3 modes), file write, empty envelope

description:
---
references:
  slice: bestiary-hf4
  reviewer: reviewer-A
  round: 1
  file: bestiary.go
  line: 31
---
## Finding

bestiary.go line 31 comment: `// NormalizedVariant is the variant suffix extracted from Family (e.g. "opus-4",`

The example value "opus-4" does not appear in any generated model. For claude-opus-4-20250514, the raw Family field is "claude-opus", so ParseFamily("claude-opus") → override → ("claude", "opus"). The "4" is a version number in the model ID, not in the Family API field.

Verified: grep 'NormalizedVariant.*opus-4' models_static_gen.go returns no results.

## Fix suggestion

Change example from "opus-4" to "opus" or use "flash-lite" which actually appears in the generated data:
```go
// NormalizedVariant is the variant suffix extracted from Family (e.g. "opus",
// "pro", "flash-lite"). Empty when the model has no variant. Populated at codegen time.
```
title: MINOR: bestiary.go doc comment example shows 'opus-4' but actual variant is 'opus'
